item.title: 作为开发人员的 12 个 API，你会喜欢它💖 | 12 APIs that you as a developer will love it 💖
item.description:
您是在开始一个新项目，学习一项新技术，还是构建一个新功能，并且您已经弄清楚了一切，您将如何实施它，除了......您将要使用的数据之外将要做什么使用？🤔

这种情况发生了很多次对吧？ 但别担心！ 我给你盖好了！ 我创建了一个我喜欢使用的酷、免费且易于使用的API 列表，我将与您分享！

希望你喜欢！ 是的，当我和我的狗一起散步时，我仍然会玩 pokemon go，所以这是我的最爱之一！ 还有谁有关系？ 吉菲

您还在等什么来为您的项目带来更多乐趣？ 开放天气

有了这个API，你就不能不知道明天会是什么天气！ 当您需要一个用于测试和原型设计的假 API 时，JSON 占位符可以满足您的需求！ 此外，他们有一个很好的例子指南。🔥⚙️👩‍💻
好的.. SWAPI 什么？ 美国国家航空航天局

既然我们在谈论星星，让我们保留主题并检查 NASA API！ 它提供来自 NASA 的真实数据，包括由 NASA 的好奇号、机遇号和精神号火星车收集的图像数据！ 这有多酷？ 不飞溅

这是救命稻草！ 多少次我们需要建立一个画廊或一个登陆页面，但我们不知道从哪里获得很棒的高质量图像？ 开发者

是的，Dev.to 有一个 API！ 如果您正在构建个人网站并想展示您超级有趣的博客文章，这非常酷！ 选择“文章”，您可以请求所有文章或仅您的文章
绝命毒师

您是《绝命毒师》的粉丝还是知道所有名言的人之一？ 然后你必须检查这个 API。 它可以让你获得报价、角色、剧集、死亡......💀🎩😎
随机数据

你想有创意并在你的项目中使用不同的东西吗？ 也许是一个让你随机获得啤酒或电脑的游戏？ 随机数据 API 为您提供了这一点，并且还模仿了 github 等omniauth 资源返回的哈希值！ 休息国家

您还可以在GitLab上查看代码并赞助所有者
瑞克和莫蒂

瑞克斯一定是疯了！ 不，它不是电池内的微型宇宙，但它非常接近！ 这个 API 让您可以按剧集、角色、地点进行查询。困难的部分是选择要查询的内容
那么...您将在下一个项目中使用哪个？😊

在评论中告诉我所有相关信息👇

克里斯蒂安·海尔曼—— 一旦暂停，monicafidalgo 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被取消。

一旦解除暂停，monicafidalgo 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，monicafidalgo 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 monicafidalgo 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Are you starting a new project, learning a new technology, or building a new feature and you already have everything figured out, how you are going to implement it, what is going to do with the exception of.. the data that you are going to use?🤔

That happens so many times right? But don't worry! I got you cover! I created a list of cool, free and easy to use APIs that I like to use, that I will share with you!

(Keynotes: API stands for “Application Programming Interface,” which is a way to communicate between different software services.)

Hope you like it! 🤗

✨1. PokéAPI

Yes, I still play pokemon go when I go for a walk with my dog, so this is one of my favorites! Who else relates? PokéAPI aims to provide a single source that has a lot of pokemon information (moves, types, abilities)!

- Needs API key: No

✨2. GIPHY

GIPHY is the world's largest GIF and video library and lets you customize a suite of powerful features. What are you waiting to bring more fun to your project? 🎡🎢🎆
- Needs API key: Yes

✨3. Open Weather

With this API, you are not allowed to not know what weather will it be tomorrow! Open Weather API collects and processes weather data from different sources such as global and local weather models, satellites, radars, and a vast network of weather stations ⛈️🥶🧊
- Needs API key: No

✨4. {JSON} Placeholder

When you need a fake API for testing and prototyping, JSON Placeholder got you cover! Also, they have a pretty good guide with examples.🔥⚙️👩‍💻
- Needs API key: No

✨5. SWAPI

Ok.. SWAPI what? SWAPI stands for "Star Wars API" and they have all the data that you need, from all seven Star Wars films!
- Needs API key: No

✨6. NASA

Since we are talking about stars, lets keep the theme and check the NASA API! It provides authentic data from NASA including image data gathered by NASA's Curiosity, Opportunity, and Spirit rovers on Mars! How cool is this? 🧑‍🚀🚀👽
- Needs API key: Yes

✨7. Unsplash

This is a life savier! How many times did we need to build a gallery or a landing page and we didn't know where to get awesome and quality images? Unsplash API is here for us!📸🌳🌼
- Needs API key: No

✨8. Dev.to

Yes, Dev.to has an API! This is pretty cool if you are building a personal website and want to display your super interesting blog posts! Choose "articles" and you can request for all articles or only yours with
curl https://dev.to/api/articles?username=yourUserName 🤯💖👀
- Needs API key: No

✨9. Breaking Bad

Are you a Breaking Bad fan and one of those who knows all the quotes? Then you must check this API. It lets you get quotes, characters, episodes, deaths... 💀🎩😎
- Needs API key: No

✨10. Random Data

Do you want to be creative and use something different on your project? Maybe a game where you get a random beer or computer? Random Data API provides you that and also mimics the hash returned by omniauth resources like github! 🐱💻🍺
- Needs API key: No

✨11. Rest Countries

Rest Countries lets you get information about countries via a RESTful API and when I mean information, is like, everything. You can also check the code on GitLab and sponsor the owner
- Needs API key: No

✨12. Rick and Morty

The Ricks Must Be Crazy! No, it's a not miniature universe inside a battery but it's pretty close! This API lets you query by episode, characters, locations.. well the difficult part will be to pick what to query
- Needs API key: No

So...Which one are you going to use in your next project?😊

Tell me all about it in the comments 👇

Christian Heilmann - Jul 1

Once suspended, monicafidalgo will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, monicafidalgo will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by monicafidalgo will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If monicafidalgo is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: monicafidalgo

item.title: JavaScript 检查对象中是否存在属性 | JavaScript check if property exists in Object
item.description:
您可能需要确定对象是否具有特定属性。

假设我们有一个用户对象。 可以选择设置电子邮件属性。 如果没有，我们希望显示一个表单，以便用户填写他们的电子邮件。

我们如何确定该字段是否存在？

 const userOne = { name : ' Chris Bongers ' , email : ' info@daily-dev-tips.com ' , }; const userTwo = { name : ' John Do ' , };

为了回答这个问题，有几种方法可以做到这一点。 让我们来看看最常见的三个。

使用 hasOwnProperty 查看对象是否有属性

通过使用hasOwnProperty ，我们可以评估对象是否具有Own属性。

让我们看看它如何处理我们的示例数据。

 console . log ( userOne . hasOwnProperty ( ' email ' )); // Returns: true console . log ( userTwo . hasOwnProperty ( ' email ' )); // Returns: false

那是完美的。 但是使用这种方法有一个问题。 它仅适用于Own属性，不适用于扩展对象属性。

您可能知道，对象带有toString方法，如果我们尝试检查它是否存在，它将返回 false。 使用 in 查看对象是否具有属性

检查对象是否具有属性的另一种更明确的方法是使用in 。

这个可以检查自己的和继承的属性。

 console . log ( ' email ' in userOne ); // Returns: true console . log ( ' email ' in userTwo ); // Returns: false console . log ( ' toString ' in userOne ); // Returns: true

使用 undefined 查看对象是否有属性

最后一种方法是使用未定义的检查。 此方法适用于省略的属性，但如果属性存在但具有未定义的值，则可能会让您头疼。

 console . log ( userOne . email !== undefined ); // Returns: true console . log ( userTwo . email !== undefined ); // Returns: false console . log ( userOne . toString !== undefined ); // Returns: true

现在让我们看看以下示例中发生了什么：

 const userThree = { name : ' Steve Stevenson ' , email : undefined , }; console . log ( userThree . email !== undefined ); // Returns: false

支票是可以接受的，但这不是我们可能要找的。

结论

当试图找出一个对象是否具有特定属性时，我们需要考虑我们想要的安全性。

我通常不建议使用undefined的检查。

如果您只评估Own属性，则hasOwnProperty是一个可靠的解决方案。

但是您可能希望安全起见并使用on检查来确定对象是否具有属性。

感谢您的阅读，让我们联系吧！

感谢您阅读我的博客。 随时订阅我的电子邮件通讯并在Facebook或Twitter 上联系



原文:

You might need to determine if an object holds a particular property.

Let's say we have a user object. Optionally the email property can be set. If not, we want to show a form so the user can fill out their email.

How can we determine if this field exists?

const userOne = { name: 'Chris Bongers', email: 'info@daily-dev-tips.com',
}; const userTwo = { name: 'John Do',
};

And to answer that, there are several ways of doing that. Let's take a look at the three most common ones.

Using hasOwnProperty to see if an object has a property

By using hasOwnProperty we can evaluate if an object has Own property.

Let's see how it would work on our example data.

console.log(userOne.hasOwnProperty('email'));
// Returns: true console.log(userTwo.hasOwnProperty('email'));
// Returns: false

That is perfect. But there is a catch to using this method. It only works for Own properties, not extended object properties.

As you may know, objects come with the toString method, and if we try to check if that exists, it will return false. (While this does exist)

console.log(userOne.toString());
// Returns: [object Object] console.log(userOne.hasOwnProperty('toString'));
// Returns false

Using in to see if an object has a property

Another more explicit way of checking if an object has a property is using in.

This one can check in own and inherited properties.

console.log('email' in userOne);
// Returns: true console.log('email' in userTwo);
// Returns: false console.log('toString' in userOne);
// Returns: true

Using undefined to see if an object has a property

The last method is to use an undefined check. This method will work for omitted properties but can cause you headaches if the property exists but has an undefined value.

console.log(userOne.email !== undefined);
// Returns: true console.log(userTwo.email !== undefined);
// Returns: false console.log(userOne.toString !== undefined);
// Returns: true

Now let's see what happens in the following example:

const userThree = { name: 'Steve Stevenson', email: undefined,
}; console.log(userThree.email !== undefined);
// Returns: false

The check is acceptable, but it's not what we might be looking for.

Conclusion

When trying to find out if an object holds a particular property, we need to consider how safe we want to be.

I would generally not recommend using the undefined check.

If you only evaluate Own properties, the hasOwnProperty is a solid solution.

But you might want to be on the safe side and use the on check to determine if an object has a property.

Thank you for reading, and let's connect!

Thank you for reading my blog. Feel free to subscribe to my email newsletter and connect on Facebook or Twitter
item.author: dailydevtips1

一旦解除暂停，本将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

原文:

Once suspended, ben will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, ben will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by ben will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If ben is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 发展 Twitter API | Evolving the Twitter API
item.description:
您是否曾经想要请求 API 功能，或在 Twitter API 中请求其他信息？ 请继续阅读，了解如何参与Open Evolution 流程。

使用 Twitter 开发者帐户注册以构建下一步

开放式建筑

自 Twitter API v2 推出以来，我们一直在做的一件事是在开放环境中构建——向开发者社区展示我们正在做的事情，并询问我们可以做哪些不同或更好的事情。 对我来说，这是在开发者关系部门工作最令人兴奋的部分之一——与每天使用平台和产品的人交谈，并将他们的想法和需求反馈给工程团队。

我们从 Twitter 开发者实验室开始，这是我们早期探索新概念（数据结构和 API 规范）的标签，这些新概念现在是 v2 的基础。 我们还一直在使用Spaces API等功能公开构建。 最近，我还在 3 月份主持了两个 Spaces 系列，讨论未来可能添加到开发者平台的内容，包括 Tweet 格式和时间线定制。

我很高兴能在 Twitter 上为开发人员开发一些非常有趣的新事物！ 在接下来的几周内，我将主持 Spaces 🟣，将您与我们的产品、工程和设计团队联系起来，讨论这些想法。 仅此工作一部分的对话启动器...

我们感谢所有加入这些空间的人！ 我们进行了一些很棒的对话，我们将继续在我们学到的东西的基础上再接再厉。

关注@TwitterDev ，留意未来加入与我们进行实时讨论的机会。 还有每月一次的开发者大会堂会议，您可以在其中了解最新更新，并询问有关 API 的问题。

还有一种方法可以直接影响 API 的技术方向： Open Evolution 过程。

需要注意的是，这是特定于 Twitter 开发者平台的（API - 不是主要的 Twitter 应用程序或网站）； 而且，它仅适用于当前版本的 API。 以前的版本可能仍然支持使用，但不会考虑添加新功能。

您可以在 GitHub 上找到存储库和工作流程。

此存储库允许您提交提案以更改 Twitter 公共 API 的各个方面。 您可以在下面以及 Twitter 开发者社区论坛上的公告帖子中阅读有关此过程的更多信息。

提交提案

任何人都可以提交提案供 Twitter 考虑。 提案必须符合定义的模板，并且必须遵守Twitter API 平台的一般原则。

在提交提案之前，我们强烈建议您通过在 GitHub 上打开问题来开始讨论，并寻求更广泛的开发者社区的共识和意见。

工作流程

  1. 分叉此存储库。
  2. 制作一个proposal-template.md的副本，并重命名它，以便标题描述提案（它可以与您的提案具有相同的标题）。 使用000-your-proposal-name.md类的格式，其中your-proposal-name是描述性名称（例如提案标题），数字部分保留000 。
  3. 充满…

如何提交提案

提案需要符合存储库中提供的特定模板； 它还需要符合Twitter API 平台的一般原则，即我们如何设计公共/外部 Twitter API 的基本规则。 你会看到这些都是围绕安全性、一致性和其他一些特性——确保详细阅读这些原则，以了解背景。

如果您不确定您的提案是否适合，您可以通过GitHub 问题或开发者论坛打开对话以开始工作，并从社区其他成员那里获得反馈。 核心流程由使用模板的拉取请求驱动，以定义需求和提案。

过程是：

  • 分叉 Open Evolution 存储库；
  • 用您的提案完成模板，命名以便描述建议的添加或更改；
  • 提交包含提案的拉取请求；
  • 等待我们团队的审核（我们的目标是尽可能在 15 个工作日内回复您，尽管这可能会因可用性而略有不同）。

提案可以被批准，也可以被拒绝。 如果审核过程获得批准，则表示原则上同意变更，但并不意味着立即实施，可能需要等待未来的开发者平台修订。

作为一个例子， Twitter 开发人员内部人员之一@IgorBrigadir提出了一个很好的建议，要求改进转推在 API 中的表示方式。 不过，您不必成为内部人员也能参与此过程！ 我们欢迎所有符合存储库中描述的标准的提案，我们喜欢了解更多关于您如何使用 API 以及您可能希望使用它构建什么的信息。

资源和后续步骤



原文:

Have you ever wanted to request an API feature, or ask for additional information in the Twitter API? Read on for information about how you can be a part of the Open Evolution process.

Sign up to build what's next, with a Twitter Developer account

Building in the open

One of the things we have been doing since the inception of Twitter API v2 is building in the open - showing the developer community what we are working on, and asking what we could be doing differently or better. For me, this is one of the most exciting parts of working in Developer Relations - talking to the people who use the platform and product every day, and representing their thoughts and needs back to the engineering teams.

We started out with Twitter Developer Labs, which was the label for our early exploration of the new concepts (data structures and API specifications) that are now foundational in v2. Labs was an early access program that enabled us to test out the new formats, and also to check that our new systems scaled.

We've also been building in the open with features like the Spaces API. Spaces are a product feature that were added while we were working on the new Twitter API, and we were able to run sessions where we asked the community what data and features would be most important in the API, and to respond to that feedback. Most recently, I also hosted two series of Spaces back in March, to talk about potential future additions to the developer platform, around Tweet formats and timeline customisation.

I'm excited to be working on some really Interesting New Things for Developers on Twitter! In the coming weeks, I'll be hosting Spaces 🟣 to connect you with our product, eng and design teams to talk about the ideas. Conversation starters for just one part of this work...

We're grateful to everyone that joined those Spaces! We had some great conversations, and we are continuing to build on what we learned.

Follow @TwitterDev to watch out for future opportunities to join live discussions with us. There's also a monthly Developer Town Hall session where you can learn about the latest updates, and ask questions about the API.

Twitter API Open Evolution

There's also a way to directly influence the technical direction of the API: the Open Evolution process.

It's important to note that this is specific to the Twitter Developer Platform (the API - not to the main Twitter app or website); and also, it is only for the current version of the API. Previous versions may remain supported for use, but new features will not be considered for addition.

You can find the repository and workflow on GitHub.

This repository allows you to submit proposals to change aspects of the Twitter public API. You can read more about this process below, and in the announcement post on the Twitter Developer Community forums.

Submitting a proposal

Anyone can submit a proposal for consideration by Twitter. Proposals must conform to a defined template, and must respect the general principles of the Twitter API platform.

Before submitting a proposal, we strongly encourage you to start a discussion by opening an issue on GitHub, and to seek consensus and input from the broader developer community.

Workflow

  1. Fork this repository.
  2. Make a copy of proposal-template.md, and rename it so that the title describes the proposal (it can have the same title as your proposal). Use a format like 000-your-proposal-name.md, where your-proposal-name is a descriptive name (e.g. the proposal title), keeping 000 for the numeric part.
  3. Fill…

How to submit a proposal

A proposal needs to conform to a specific template, provided in the repository; and it also needs to align with the general principles of the Twitter API platform, the fundamental rules of how we approach design of the public / external Twitter API. You'll see that these are around security, consistency, and a few other features - make sure to read the principles in detail, to understand the background.

If you're not sure about how your proposal would fit, you can open a conversation via GitHub Issues or on the developer forums to get things started, and to get feedback from other members of the community. The core process is driven by a Pull Request using the template, to define the requirement and proposal.

The process is:

  • fork the Open Evolution repository;
  • complete the template with your proposal, named so that it describes the suggested addition or change;
  • submit a Pull Request containing the proposal;
  • await a review from our team (we aim to get back to you within 15 working days if possible, although this may vary slightly with availability).

Proposals can be approved, or rejected. If the review process results in approval, this means that the changes are agreed in principle, but does not mean that they will be immediately implemented, and they may have to wait for a future developer platform revision.

As an example of how this can go, one of the Twitter Developer Insiders, @IgorBrigadir, made a great proposal requesting improvements to the way that Retweets are represented in the API. You do not have to be an Insider to get involved in the process, though! We welcome all proposals that meet the criteria described in the repository, and we love to learn more about how you use the API and what you might want to build with it.

Resources and next steps
item.author: andypiper

item.title: 过去一周的 7 大特色 DEV 帖子 | Top 7 Featured DEV Posts from the Past Week
item.description:
开发团队的格雷西·格雷戈里（她/她）

发表于每个星期二，我们都会根据流量、参与度和一些编辑策划来汇总前一周的热门帖子。 典型的一周从周一开始，到周日结束，但不用担心，我们会考虑本周晚些时候发布的帖子。

反思与学习

超级有帮助！

让我们来谈谈 <!DOCTYPE html>！

恭喜他们在 DEV 上发表第一篇文章！

可视化模式

祝贺@eevajonnapanula发布了这篇很棒的#showdev 帖子——更重要的是，感谢他们新的毛衣设计网站！

可访问性：什么、为什么和如何

感谢@yuridevat解释什么是可访问性、为什么它很重要以及我们如何实现可访问性设计。

码头工人入门

我们都可以在某一时刻从这个合理的建议中受益！

有趣的设计

这就是我们本周二的每周前 7 名！ 请关注本周的 dev.to 以获取每日内容和讨论……并确保在未来关注这个系列。 你可能只是在里面！

一旦暂停，devteam 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，devteam 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，devteam 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 devteam 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Gracie Gregory (she/her) for The DEV Team

Posted on Jul 5

Every Tuesday we round up the previous week's top posts based on traffic, engagement, and a hint of editorial curation. The typical week starts on Monday and ends on Sunday, but don't worry, we take into account posts that are published later in the week.

Reflecting and learning

@princewhyte2 looked back on everything they've learned so far as a programmer and summarized what they wish thy knew when they were starting out. Super helpful!

Let's talk about <!DOCTYPE html>!

@aman894 helps us understand the importance of the statement. Congrats to them on their first post on DEV!

Visualizing pattens

Congrats to @eevajonnapanula for this awesome #showdev post — and more importantly, for their new sweater design site!

"Git commit messages are how we communicate to our future selves."

@simeg explains why git commit messages are important and how you can improve yours.

Accessibility: the what, why and how

Thank you to @yuridevat for explaining what accessibility is, why it's important and how we can achieve accessible design.

Docker Primer

@roselinebassey explains the basics that can help you get started with Docker. We all could benefit from this sound advice at one point or another!

Fun with design

@riapacheco shows us how to create a line chart "in a style that's all the rage in cryptocurrency dashboard design and optimized for user interaction."

That's it for our weekly Top 7 for this Tuesday! Keep an eye on dev.to this week for daily content and discussions...and be sure to keep an eye on this series in the future. You might just be in it!

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Once suspended, devteam will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, devteam will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by devteam will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If devteam is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: graciegregory

item.title: 我连续 9 年被授予 Microsoft MVP | I have been awarded for 9 years in a row as Microsoft MVP
item.description:
伊曼纽尔·巴托莱西

发表于多年来，7 月 1 日（今年的日期被推迟到截止日期前几个小时）对我来说是一个特殊的日子。 每年的这一天，我们都在等待 Microsoft MVP 再续一年。

之所以称为“F5 日”，是因为我们正在等待奖励的确认邮件🙂

对我来说，这是连续第 9 年，但每次都像是第一次。
我为能成为这个家庭的一员这么久而感到自豪和荣幸，并希望能成为这个家庭的一份子更久！

用数字说话

今年我试图用一些客观的数字来总结我的活动。

  • 年度最佳时刻

    这是我在 Codemotion 意大利的主题演讲的延时摄影，连接了超过 1000,000 人

    在这次会议之后，我收到了很多关于我的设置和我用于在线会议的工具的问题。
    这就是为什么几天后我在一次会议期间写了一篇关于我的设置的博客文章的原因。

    如果您想进一步了解我的一般设置，请单击下面的图片以了解我当前的设置。

    结论

    我认为9年是一个很好的成就，但现在我想达到10年的目标。
    当然，我刚刚开始研究它！

    迪普·K·萨西哈兰—— 一旦暂停，kasuken 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，kasuken 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，kasuken 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 kasuken 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    Emanuele Bartolesi

    Posted on Jul 5

    Since many years, the 1st of July (this year the date was postponed a few hours before the due date) is a special day for me. It's the day each year we wait for renewal as Microsoft MVP for another year.

    It's call "The F5 day" because we are waiting for the confirmation email of the award 🙂

    For me it's the 9th year in a row, but each time is like the first time.
    I am proud and honored to have been a part of this family for so long and hope to be a part of it for much longer!

    Talking by numbers

    This year I tried to summarize my activities with some objective numbers.

    • ✍️ 47 blog posts (80+ since 2020)
    • 🗣️ 33 events as a speaker
    • 🧑‍💻 4 events in person
    • 👑 3 keynote
    • 🎥 6 events as a producer/organizer
    • 🎞️ 100+ hours on Twitch (even I didn't stream since January 2022)
    • 🎙️ 6 interviews as guest

    Best moment of the year

    This is the timelapse of my keynote at Codemotion Italy with more than 1000 thousand people connected

    After this session I received a lot of questions about my setup and the tools I use for online sessions.
    This is the reason why after a few days, I wrote a blog post about my setup during a conference.

    If you want to know more about my setup in general instead, click on the picture below to find out about my current setup.

    Conclusion

    I think 9 years are a good achievement, but now I'd like to get to the 10-year mark.
    And of course, I just started to work on it!

    Deepu K Sasidharan - Jul 5

    Once suspended, kasuken will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

    Once unsuspended, kasuken will be able to comment and publish posts again.

    Once unpublished, all posts by kasuken will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

    If kasuken is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: kasuken

item.title: 滑块就像洋葱：单个滑块的三个渐变层 | Sliders are like onions: three gradient layers of a single slider
item.description:
我们的Taiga UI库包含数百个有用的组件、指令和服务。 代码可维护性是我们 UI Kit 库的关键问题。 这就是为什么我们总是尝试尽可能少地编写代码并寻找原生解决方案。

在本文中，我会告诉你这样的例子——使用内置浏览器工具和尽可能少的 JavaScript 代码开发 Angular 组件Slider 。 您将了解可访问性、具有多层渐变背景的有趣解决方案，甚至还有一些关于 Angular 中的更改检测的内容。

不可能这么难吧？

我们的任务是开发一个 UI 元素，它可以帮助用户从有序范围中选择一个数字。 输入由两部分组成：

  • 用户可以水平移动它来增加或减少选择的数字
  • 乍一看，这项任务似乎很简单。 监听MouseMove / TouchMove事件，计算它们发生的距离轨道起点的距离，将滑块移动到那里，最后为用户提供一个数值。

    由于它的简单性，甚至可能错误地认为将这篇文章专门用于这个主题是没有意义的。 但是，在做出这样的结论之前，我们必须深入研究所有细节。 让我们从熟悉 W3C 文档开始。

    为了让事情不那么抽象，想象一个人访问一个网站并打开一个模式对话框。 您想直观地按下哪个键盘键来关闭此对话框？ 它是一个 Esc 按钮。 从这个意义上说，如果 Web 应用程序是由经验丰富的团队开发的，那么当您按下此键时，弹出窗口将关闭。

    此示例说明了 W3C 原则之一，让我们深入了解有关滑块的 W3C 指南：

    键盘导航。 按下ArrowUp / ArrowDown和ArrowRight / ArrowLeft键应该（分别）将滑块的值增加/减少一步。 按Home / End应将滑块设置为其范围内的第一个/最后一个允许值。 此外，还有关于键PageUp / PageDown的建议：它们应该将滑块值增加/减少一个大于ArrowUp / ArrowDown所做的步长变化的量。 如果您开发自己的滑块，您应该准备好为提到的具有不同行为的键编写 8 个事件处理程序。

    可访问性。 任何网站都可以供残障人士使用，例如，用户在使用键盘和鼠标时可能会遇到问题。 在这种情况下，会使用语音识别和其他辅助技术。 然而，用户向计算机宣布它应该在屏幕上做什么，因为该网站应该被正确标记。

    回到我们的任务，让我们看看可访问滑块的要求：

    • 用作可聚焦滑块控件的元素具有role="slider"
    • 滑块元素的aria-valuenow属性设置为滑块的当前值
    • 滑块元素的aria-valuemin / aria-valuemax属性设置为滑块的最小/最大值允许值
    • 如果aria-valuenow的值对用户不友好，则aria-valuetext属性设置为使滑块值易于理解的字符串。 最后，我们应该始终牢记aria-label ！

    配置滑块步骤的可能性。 例如，开发人员可以限制只选择整数值。 此外，它们可能允许用户输入具有0.0001精度的值。

    考虑到所有细微差别和可访问性原则可能会被视为大量额外工作，这就是为什么一切都不像乍看起来那么简单。

    但是浏览器已经做到了！ 有一个内置的<input type="range" />滑块，它遵循所有提到的标准。 这样的内置解决方案并不花哨，甚至在每个浏览器中看起来都不一样，这对任何人来说都不足为奇。 因此，我将向您展示如何根据需要自定义内置滑块以确保跨浏览器支持。

    我们将为 Angular 编写组件。 但是该解决方案包含的 Javascript 代码非常少，您可以轻松地将其转移到您喜欢的框架中。

    如果您在 DevTools 中启用了 shadow DOM（例如，在 Chrome 的设置中，可以找到“显示用户代理 shadow DOM”选项），您会注意到内置的<input type="range" />不是单一的标签。 它内部包含其他标签（但我们没有添加它们！）。 此外，嵌套标签的内部结构在不同的浏览器中可能会有所不同。 在某个地方你可以找到一个带有 3 个div容器的扁平结构，在某个地方——结构的额外深度。

    每个浏览器引擎都有不同的方法来编写自己的滑块，因此它们都有不同的结构。 但基本概念是相同的：总是有一个带有滑块轨道的标签和一个带有拇指的容器。 您只需要知道如何访问这些嵌套标签。

    您不能通过组合 CSS 选择器（例如input[type="range"] > div ）访问<input />中的 HTML 标记。 所有滑块标记都封装在 shadow DOM 中，无法从外部访问。 但是浏览器提供了 API（伪类），您可以通过它访问必要的标签。

    所有带有 WebKit/Blink 引擎的浏览器（例如 Safari 和 Chrome）都有这样的结构：

    这就是编写自己的简单滑块所需要知道的全部内容。 但是，如果您需要更大的灵活性，比标签着色更复杂的东西，您将面临一些问题。 我会告诉你可能出现的问题以及如何处理它们。

    这会很有趣：写一个基本的滑块

    首先，让我们使用之前的信息来定制我们滑块的基本版本。

    为代码的自文档声明一些较小的变量：

    声明less-mixin以自定义滑块的轨道。 现在它很简单，但在接下来的部分中会大大扩展。

    我们还需要 mixin 来自定义滑块的拇指：

    如果您不太了解& -symbol 的工作原理，我建议您阅读此Less-documentation 部分。

    最后，让我们为 webkit 浏览器创建一个特殊的 mixin。 如果滑块的拇指大于/小于滑块轨道的高度，我们需要它。 多种垂直居中内容的方法在这里无济于事，但可以通过以下方式解决问题：

    现在您可以创建一个 Angular 组件。 我们的组件没有额外的布局，但它有一个带有原生元素<input type="range" />的属性选择器。 使用属性选择器是增强原生元素行为的好习惯。 官方 Angular 文档声称：“在创作 Angular 组件时，您应该尽可能直接重用这些原生元素，而不是重新实现支持良好的行为”。 您可以在我们的Taiga UI 库中找到许多此类示例。

    该组件使用更改检测策略OnPush 。 如果你不知道或者犹豫使用它，我建议阅读我同事的文章：

    我们组件的打字稿文件包含：

    我们组件的选择器强制用户指定输入类型（type="range"）。 您可以建议改善开发人员体验并通过组件元数据中的host -property 设置此静态属性。 但不幸的是，这破坏了 Angular 团队的内置 controlValueAccessor的逻辑，该逻辑通过input[type=range] CSS 选择器应用。

    在 Less 文件中，我们将所有之前创建的 mixins 应用到:host – 选择器（只是提醒一下：在这种情况下，它是<input type="range" /> – 元素）：

    瞧！ 我们有一个用于自定义内置滑块的基本跨浏览器解决方案。 但目前还有更多的细微差别被忽视。

    不幸的是，WebKit/Blink 浏览器没有这样的功能。 但是线性梯度可以解决这个问题。

    以前，我们只是将整个滑块轨道填充为灰色。 现在，我们将用进度的颜色填充部分轨道，并将其余部分保留为灰色。

    我们将一些简单的数学添加到组件的类中。 它将计算滑块填充的百分比，并将此值保存到--slider-fill-percentage CSS-variable ：

    现在，我们需要改进之前创建的 mixin customize-track ：

    让我们尝试一下生成的组件：

     <input type= "range" tuiSlider value= "30" />

    我们正在使用changeDetectionStrategy.OnPush ，因此当用户移动滑块的拇指时，预计滑块不会重新计算--slider-fill-percentage CSS 变量的值。 我们需要告诉 Angular 何时运行变更检测。 实现这一点很简单：我们需要在InputEvent触发时触发更改检测。 我们在组件元数据中设置了一个空的事件处理程序：

    现在，当用户与滑块交互时，会重新计算--slider-fill-percentage CSS 变量的值。

    少了一个问题，现在我们的滑块在所有现代浏览器中都是完全相同的。

    通过多层渐变添加刻度

    我们有一个可行的解决方案。 假设我们想要使用具有以下原生属性组合的滑块：

     <input type= "range" tuiSlider min= "0" max= "100" step= "20" />

    这种组合意味着用户只能将滑块移动一个 20 的倍数的步长。用户只能在范围内选择五个值，或者换句话说，滑块由五个段组成。 对于这种情况，一种流行的做法是通过滑块轨道上的刻度/标记直观地突出显示片段。

    我们可以尝试通过输入中的附加（伪）元素来实现这一点。 但是只有 webkit 浏览器支持这种行为，因为它不是一个普遍接受的标准。 另一种选择是尝试通过渐变来实现刻度，但是在上一章中，渐变已经用于实现 webkit 浏览器的进度指示器（所以这个地方似乎已经被占用了）。

    不用担心！ 有一个解决方案：渐变可以在几层中相互堆叠。 我们可以使用repeating-linear-gradient创建一个新层，并将其应用到前一层之上。

    首先，让我们将这些行添加到组件代码中：

    我们添加了新的输入属性segments ，开发人员可以使用它来设置要在滑块轨道上显示的可视段的数量。 组件的新 getter 执行一个简单的数学运算——它计算每个段应该占据总长度的百分之几，并将结果保存在--slider-segment-width CSS 变量中。

    仍然需要升级.customize-track() mixin。 要将多个渐变层应用于同一背景，只需在background-image属性中用逗号分隔它们。 例如：

    最重要的是不要混淆图层彼此堆叠的顺序。 第一个指定的渐变将叠加在所有其他渐变之上，最后一个将成为背景的底层。 换句话说，层的堆叠从列表枚举的末尾开始。

    所有其他background-*属性都有类似的行为。 您可以为所有图层仅指定一组参数，也可以列出以逗号分隔的参数（与background-image属性中的顺序相同）来为每个图层设置自己的值。

    原生滑块的一个重要特性是，当用户将拇指移动到轨道的开始/结束时，拇指接触到的不是轨道的中心，而是它的边缘。 因此，滑块的拇指永远不会超出滑块轨道的视觉边界。 因此，第一个标记不是从曲目的最开头开始，而是有一个移位，这允许它位于拇指的中心（当它在最左边的位置时），并且也正确地放置了其余的分数。

    除了background-image层，我们还为background-color设置了一个值。 这里的逻辑如下： background-color作为最后一个 fallback 层。 没有被任何层覆盖的轨道的整个表面将填充为此属性值的颜色。

    就这样。 这个解决方案很好地说明了有时如何在没有额外嵌套 HTML 标记的情况下解决前端任务。

    包起来

    我在 StackBlitz 示例中复制了滑块的最终版本：

    在Taiga UI 库中，我们使用完全相同的解决方案，并进行了细微的改进。 在Slider 组件的展示中，您可以看到其使用示例。

    发明自己的解决方案并不总是必要的——有时，我们可以重复使用本地替代方案，只需稍加修改。 当您忽略内置解决方案时，您会扩展您的代码库，从而增加其支持的复杂性。 难怪有一种现代智慧说：最容易维护的代码是从未编写过的代码。



原文:

Our library Taiga UI contains hundreds of useful components, directives, and services. The code maintainability is a pivotal issue for our UI Kit library. That is why we always try to write as little code as possible and look for native solutions.

In the article, I will tell you about such example – the development of Angular-component Slider using built-in browser tools and as few lines of JavaScript as possible. You will read about accessibility, an interesting solution with multi-layer gradient background, and even a bit about Change Detection in Angular.

It cannot be this hard, right?

We have a task to develop a UI-element which helps users to select a number from an ordered range. Input consists of two parts:

  • thumb – the interactive element of the input. Users can move it horizontally to increase or decrease the selected number
  • track – the space along which the thumb moves

At first glance, the task seems simple. Listen to MouseMove / TouchMove events, calculate the distance how far from the beginning of the track they occurred, move the slider to there, and, finally, provide the user with a numerical value.

It could even mistakenly seem that there is no sense in devoting the article to this subject because of its simplicity. However, we have to look into all the in-depth details before making such a conclusion. Let’s kick it off by getting acquainted with W3C documentation.

W3C is a worldwide organization that maintains standards for the development of web applications and its primary goal is to make the Web accessible and understandable. To make things less abstract, imagine that one visits a website and opens a modal dialog. Which keyboard key would you intuitively like to press to close this dialog? It is an Esc button. In this sense, if a web application is developed by an experienced team, then the popup will close when you press this key.

This example illustrates one the W3C principles, let’s dive deeper into W3C guidelines about sliders:

Keyboard navigation. Pressed ArrowUp/ArrowDown and ArrowRight/ArrowLeft keys should increase/decrease (respectively) the value of the slider by one step. Pressing Home/End should set the slider to the first/last allowed value in its range. Also, there are recommendations about keys PageUp/PageDown: they should increase/decrease the slider value by an amount larger than the step change made by ArrowUp/ArrowDown. If you develop your own slider, you should be ready to write 8 event handlers for the mentioned keys with different behaviors.

Accessibility. Any website can be used by people with disabilities, for instance, a user may experience issues using the keyboard and mouse. In such cases, voice-recognition and other assistive technologies are used. The user pronounces to the computer what it should do on the screen, however, for that the website should be correctly marked up.

Moving back to our task, let’s see the requirements for an accessible slider:

  • The element serving as the focusable slider control has role="slider"
  • The slider element has the aria-valuenow property set to the current value of the slider
  • The slider element has the aria-valuemin / aria-valuemax property set to the minimum/maximum allowed value of the slider
  • If the value of aria-valuenow is not user-friendly, the aria-valuetext property is set to a string that makes the slider value understandable. A simple example from the W3C’s documentation: if you decide to use a slider to select the days of the week (from the first to the seventh), don't forget to provide the slider with a text description of the selected value (e.g., "Monday")
  • Finally, we should always keep aria-label in mind!

Possibility to configure the slider step. For instance, a developer can restrict to selecting only integer values. Also, they may allow the user to input values with 0.0001-precision.

Considering all the nuances and accessibility principles might be perceived as tons of extra work, that is why everything is not as simple as it might seem at first glance.

But the browser has already done it! There is a built-in <input type="range" /> slider that follows all mentioned standards. It is not surprising to anyone that such a built-in solution is not fancy, and even looks different in each browser. Therefore, I will show you how to customize the built-in slider to one’s needs to ensure cross-browser support.

We will write the component for Angular. But the solution contains so little Javascript code that you can easily transfer it to your favorite framework.

Anatomy of <input type="range" />

If you enable the shadow DOM in your DevTools (e.g., in Chrome’s settings, one can find option “Show user agent shadow DOM”), you can notice that the built-in <input type="range" /> is not a single tag. It contains other tags inside (but we did not add them!). Also, the internal structure of nested tags may vary in different browsers. Somewhere you can find a flat structure with 3 div-containers, and somewhere – additional depth of the structure.

Each browser engine has a different approach to writing its own slider, so they all have a different structure. But the basic concepts are the same: there is always a tag with a slider track and a container with a thumb. You just need to know how to access these nested tags.

You can't access the HTML tags inside an <input /> via combinator CSS selector (for instance, input[type="range"] > div). All slider markup is encapsulated inside the shadow DOM and is not accessible from the outside. But browsers provide API (pseudo-classes) via which you can reach the necessary tags.

All browsers with the WebKit/Blink engines (e.g., Safari and Chrome) have this anatomy:

Firefox gives more opportunities for customization: besides the pseudo-classes for the track and the thumb, there is an additional pseudo-class to customize the progress indicator of the slider.

That's all you need to know to write your own simple slider. However, if you need more flexibility, something more complex than tag coloring, you will face some problems. I'll tell you about possible problems and how to deal with them.

This’ll be fun: writing a basic slider

Firstly, let's use previous information to customize the basic version of our slider.

Declare some less-variables for self-documentation of the code:

Declare less-mixin to customize track of the slider. It will be simple for now, but it will be considerably extended in the following sections.

We also need mixin to customize the thumb of the slider:

If you don't quite understand how the &-symbol works, I advise you to read this Less-documentation section.

Finally, let’s create a special mixin only for webkit browsers. We need it if the slider’s thumb is larger/smaller than the height of the slider track. Webkit browsers don’t align it to the center. Multiple ways to vertically center the content won't help here, but the problem can be solved in this way:

Now you can create an Angular–component. Our component has no additional layout, but it has an attribute selector with the native element <input type="range" />. Using attribute selector is a good practice to augment the behavior of native elements. Official Angular documentation claims: "When authoring Angular components, you should re-use these native elements directly when possible, rather than re-implementing well-supported behaviors". You can find many such examples in our Taiga UI library.

The component uses a change-detection strategy OnPush. If you don’t know it or hesitate to use it, I recommend reading the article of my colleague:

Typescript-file of our component contains:

Our component's selector forces the user to specify the input type (type="range"). You could suggest improving the developer experience and set this static property through host-property in the component's metadata. But unfortunately, this breaks the logic of the built-in controlValueAccessor from the Angular team, which is applied via the input[type=range] CSS selector.

In the Less file, we apply all previously created mixins to the :host – selector (just a reminder: in this case, it is <input type="range" />– element):

Voilà! We have got a basic cross-browser solution for customizing the built-in slider. But there are more nuances that are currently overlooked.

Webkit customization problems with the progress of the slider

Firefox takes care of the developer's desire to customize the progress indicator of the slider – the moz-range-progress pseudo-class. Unfortunately, there is no such functionality for WebKit/Blink browsers. But linear gradients can solve this problem.

Previously, we just filled the entire slider track in gray. And now, we will fill part of the track in the color of progress, and leave the rest of it gray.

We add some simple math to the class of the component. It will calculate how many percents the slider are filled, and save this value to the --slider-fill-percentage CSS-variable:

Now, we need to improve the previously created mixin customize-track:

Let's try the resulting component in action:

<input type="range" tuiSlider value="30" />

We're using changeDetectionStrategy.OnPush, so it's expected that the slider doesn’t recalculate the value of the --slider-fill-percentage CSS-variable while the user moves the slider's thumb. We need to tell Angular when to run the change detection. Achieving this is simple: we need to trigger change detection when the InputEvent fires. We set an empty event handler in the component metadata:

Now, the value of the --slider-fill-percentage CSS-variable is recalculated when the user interacts with the slider.

One problem less, and now our slider is completely identical in all modern browsers.

Adding ticks via multi-layer gradient

We have a working solution. Let's imagine that we wanted to use a slider with the following combination of native attributes:

<input type="range" tuiSlider min="0" max="100" step="20"/>

This combination means that the user can only move the slider by a step that is a multiple of 20. Users can only select five values in the range, or in other words, the slider consists of five segments. A popular practice for such a case is to highlight segments visually through ticks/marks on the slider track.

We can try to achieve this via additional (pseudo-)elements inside the input. But only webkit browsers support this behavior, because it's not a commonly accepted standard. Another option is to try to implement the ticks through a gradient, but in the last chapter, a gradient was already used to implement a progress indicator for webkit browsers (so the place seems to be already taken).

No worries! There is a solution: gradients can be stacked on each other in several layers. We can create a new layer using repeating-linear-gradient and apply it on top of the previous one.

Firstly, let's add these lines to the component code:

We have added new input-property segments, with which the developer can set the number of visual segments to be shown on the slider track. And the new getter of the component performs a simple math operation – it calculates how many percent of the total length each segment should occupy and saves the result in the --slider-segment-width CSS-variable.

It remains to upgrade the .customize-track() mixin. To apply several gradient layers to the same background, just separate them by commas in the background-image property. For example:

The main thing is not to get confused in what order the layers stack on each other. The first specified gradient will be layered on top of all others, and the last one will become the bottom layer of the background. In other words, the stacking of layers on top of each other begins at the end of the list enumeration.

All other background-* properties have a similar behavior. You can specify only one set of parameters for all layers, or you can list the parameters separated by commas (in the same order as in the background-image property) to set your own values for each layer.

Upgraded version of mixin customize-track:

An important feature of native sliders is that when the user moves the thumb to the start/end of the track, the thumb touches the end of the track not with its center, but with its edge. So, the thumb of the slider never goes beyond the visual boundaries of the slider track. Therefore, the first mark does not start at the very beginning of the track, but with a shift, which allows it to be in the center of the thumb (when it is in the leftmost position), and also correctly places the rest of the marks.

In addition to the background-image layers, we also set a value for background-color. The logic here is as follows: background-color acts as the last fallback layer. The entire surface of the track, which is not covered by any of the layers, will be filled in the color of this property’s value.

That's all. This solution is a good illustration of how sometimes a frontend-task can be solved without additional nested HTML tags.

Wrapping up

I reproduced the final version of the slider in StackBlitz example:

In the Taiga UI library, we use exactly the same solution with minor improvements. On the showcase of the Slider component, you can see examples of its usage.

It is not always necessary to invent your own solutions – sometimes, we can re-use native alternatives with little modifications. When you ignore a built-in solution, you expand your codebase, increasing the complexity of its support. No wonder one modern wisdom says: the code easiest to maintain is the code that was never written.
item.author: nsbarsukov

item.title: 加快 PostgreSQL 中的批量加载 | Speeding Up Bulk Loading in PostgreSQL
item.description:
测试 4 种将数据批量加载到 PostgreSQL 中的方法

极品飞车

如果您只需要将几百条记录加载到数据库中，您可能不太关心效率。 但是当尝试插入数千甚至数百万条记录时会发生什么？ 现在，数据加载效率可能意味着项目成功与失败之间的差异，或者至少是及时交付项目与严重逾期项目之间的差异。

但是，如果您的数据专门在 CSV（或二进制）文件中，这只是一个很好的解决方案。 但是，如果您需要从纯 SQL 加载数据怎么办？ 那么最快的方法是什么？

四种插入数据的方法

基本插入命令

让我们看看一些基本的 SQL 插入命令的结构：

 create table users ( id integer , firstname text , lastname text ); insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 1 , 'George' , 'Washington' ); insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 2 , 'John' , 'Adams' ); insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 3 , 'Thomas' , 'Jefferson' );

现在我们有一些基本的 SQL 用于将记录插入到我们的用户表中。 这会将数据放入我们的表中，好吧，但这是将数据放入我们的表中最慢的方法。 让我们看看我们可以加快速度的一些方法。

交易

加快速度的一种快速简便的方法是将大批量的插入语句放入事务中：

 begin transaction ; insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 1 , 'George' , 'Washington' ); insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 2 , 'John' , 'Adams' ); insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 3 , 'Thomas' , 'Jefferson' ); commit ;

在我的 Windows 测试中，这将 100k 用户记录的插入速度提高了一倍。 在 MacOS 下，速度翻了三倍。 虽然您可以在技术上创建包含数十亿条记录的批次，但您可能希望尝试使用 1000、10000、100000 或类似的批次大小，以根据您的硬件、带宽和记录查看最有效的方法尺寸。

批量插入

另一种加快速度的方法是在进行插入时使用 SQL 批量插入语法。 例如：

 insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 1 , 'George' , 'Washington' ), ( 2 , 'John' , 'Adams' ), ( 3 , 'Thomas' , 'Jefferson' );

这种方法大大加快了速度。 在我的测试中，它快了大约 6 倍。 与事务相同的规则适用于批量大小——您需要测试不同的批量大小以优化事物。 对于大多数应用程序，我通常倾向于从一批大约 10000 条记录开始，如果效果足够好，我就把它留在那里。

两者怎么样？

您可以将事务和批量插入语句结合起来以提高速度吗？ 嗯，是的，也不是。 您当然可以将它们结合起来，但速度的提高可以忽略不计（或者在我的 Windows 测试用例中，它甚至会稍微减慢速度。）

 begin transaction ; insert into users ( id , firstname , lastname ) values ( 1 , 'George' , 'Washington' ), ( 2 , 'John' , 'Adams' ), ( 3 , 'Thomas' , 'Jefferson' ); commit ;

因此，虽然在这里使用这两种技术是完全有效的，但它可能不是加载数据的最快方式。

缺点

使用事务或批量插入的潜在缺点是什么？ 错误处理是主要的。 如果批处理中的任何一条记录失败，则整个批处理都将失败，并且不会从该批处理中将任何数据插入到您的表中。 所以你需要确保你的数据是有效的，否则有一些方法可以分解和修复失败的批次。

如果失败是由唯一约束引起的，您可以在插入语句中使用on conflict子句，但如果您的插入因任何其他原因失败，它将丢弃整个批次。

其他速度注意事项

还有许多其他因素会影响您的数据插入速度以及使事情变得更快的方法。 在插入数据之前删除索引、创建非日志表以及避免不必要的唯一键只是其中的一小部分。 这些其他优化将提高性能，但可能不如这里描述的基本技术那么显着。

结论

如果您需要处理大量数据，那么在编写 SQL 插入代码时提前计划是值得的。 一些小的更改可能会为您节省数小时（有时甚至数天）的处理时间。

附录：样本测试结果

有关测试这些方法的一些代码，请参阅我的 GitHub 存储库postgresql-bulk-load-tests 。 我的测试运行结果如下所示。


 =========================== Windows VM (UTM Windows 11) =========================== create 100k users with individual insert statements 30.0 seconds create 100k users with individual insert statements in a transaction 14.0 seconds create 100k users with batch insert statement 4.3 seconds create 100k users with batch insert statement in a transaction 4.6 seconds ==================== MacBook Pro (M1 Max) ==================== create 100k users with individual insert statements real 0m9.112s user 0m0.509s sys 0m0.337s create 100k users with individual insert statements in a transaction real 0m2.540s user 0m0.457s sys 0m0.325s create 100k users with batch insert statement real 0m1.360s user 0m0.179s sys 0m0.042s create 100k users with batch insert statement in a transaction real 0m1.154s user 0m0.189s sys 0m0.041s


原文:

Testing 4 ways to bulk load data into PostgreSQL

The Need For Speed

If you only need to load a few hundred records into your database, you probably aren't too concerned about efficiency. But what happens when try to insert thousands, or even millions of records? Now, data-loading efficiency can mean the difference between success and failure for your project, or at the very least the difference between a project that’s delivered timely and one that’s woefully overdue.

PostgreSQL has a great copy command that’s optimized for this task: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/current/sql-copy.html. But that’s only a good solution if your data is specifically in a CSV (or Binary) file. But what if you need to load data from pure SQL? Then what’s the fastest way?

Four Ways to Insert Data

Basic Insert Commands

Let’s look at the structure for some basic SQL insert commands:

create table users (id integer, firstname text, lastname text);
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (1, 'George', 'Washington');
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (2, 'John', 'Adams');
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (3, 'Thomas', 'Jefferson');

Now we have some basic SQL for inserting records into our user table. This will get the data into our table, alright, but it's the slowest way to get data into our table. Let's look at some ways we can speed things up.

Transactions

A quick and easy way to speed things up is simply to put large batches of insert statements inside a transaction:

begin transaction;
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (1, 'George', 'Washington');
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (2, 'John', 'Adams');
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (3, 'Thomas', 'Jefferson');
commit;

In my Windows test, this doubled the speed of the insert of 100k user records. Under MacOS, the speed tripled. While you can technically create batches with billions of records in them, you'll probably want to experiment with batch sizes of, say 1000, 10000, 100000, or something like that to see what works best based on your hardware, bandwidth, and record size.

Batched Inserts

Another way to speed things up is to use the SQL batch insert syntax when doing your insert. For example:

insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (1, 'George', 'Washington'), (2, 'John', 'Adams'), (3, 'Thomas', 'Jefferson');

This method speeds things up considerably. In my tests, it was about 6 times faster. The same rules apply to batch sizes as with transactions -- you'll want to test different batch sizes to optimize things. I generally tend to start with a batch of around 10000 records for most applications and if that works well enough, I leave it there.

What About Both?

Can you combine transactions and batch insert statements for even more speed? Well, yes, and no. You certainly can combine them, but the speed increase is negligible (or in my Windows test case it even slowed things down just a bit.)

begin transaction;
insert into users (id, firstname, lastname) values (1, 'George', 'Washington'), (2, 'John', 'Adams'), (3, 'Thomas', 'Jefferson');
commit;

So, while using both techniques here is perfectly valid, it may not be the fastest way to load data.

The Downsides

What are the potential downsides of using transactions or batched inserts? Error handling is the main one. If any one of the records in your batch fails, the entire batch will fail and no data will be inserted into your table from that batch. So you'll need to make sure your data is valid or else have some way to break up and fix failed batches.

If the failure is caused by a unique constraint, you can use the on conflict clause in your insert statement, but if your insert fails for any other reason, it'll throw out the whole batch.

Other Speed Considerations

There are many other factors that can affect your data insert speed and ways you can make things even faster. Removing indexes until after inserting data, creating non-logged tables, and avoiding unnecessary unique keys are just a few of these. These other optimizations will improve performance, but probably not nearly as dramatically as the basic techniques described here.

Conclusion

If you need to deal with large amounts of data, it pays to plan ahead when you're writing your SQL insert code. A few small changes can potentially save you hours (or sometimes even days) of processing time.

Appendix: Sample Test Results

See my GitHub Repo postgresql-bulk-load-tests for some code to test these methods. My test run results are listed below.


===========================
Windows VM (UTM Windows 11)
===========================
create 100k users with individual insert statements
30.0 seconds
create 100k users with individual insert statements in a transaction
14.0 seconds
create 100k users with batch insert statement
4.3 seconds
create 100k users with batch insert statement in a transaction
4.6 seconds

====================
MacBook Pro (M1 Max)
====================
create 100k users with individual insert statements

real    0m9.112s
user    0m0.509s
sys     0m0.337s

create 100k users with individual insert statements in a transaction

real    0m2.540s
user    0m0.457s
sys     0m0.325s

create 100k users with batch insert statement

real    0m1.360s
user    0m0.179s
sys     0m0.042s

create 100k users with batch insert statement in a transaction

real    0m1.154s
user    0m0.189s
sys     0m0.041s
item.author: burggraf

item.title: 使用 ReactJS 设置 Laravel 9 项目 | Setup Laravel 9 project with ReactJS
item.description:
自 Laravel 9 以来，Laravel 中包含了许多新功能和改进。 此外，开发者体验也得到了改善，尤其是引入了前端资产捆绑器Vite 。 将 React 集成到 Laravel 9 中发生了一些变化，Vite 作为新的前端资产捆绑器。 我之前写过一篇关于如何将 ReactJS 集成到 Laravel的文章，但它适用于 Laravel 8.x 及之前版本，或者任何使用 webpack 编译前端的人。 在本教程中，我们将介绍如何使用 Laravel 9 做到这一点。

本教程将引导我们从头开始将 ReactJS 集成到 Laravel。 您也可以使用入门套件来做到这一点。

先决条件：

设置后端

我们可以使用 Laravel 安装程序或使用 composer来做到这一点。 我们将使用 Laravel 安装程序。 在终端中键入以下命令：

 laravel new laravel-react

让我们进入我们的新项目：

我们将需要InertiaJS来帮助我们在一个项目中包含这两个堆栈。 我们正在使用 composer 安装它。 将惯性视为将前端堆栈粘到后端堆栈的粘合剂。 在您的终端中，键入：

 composer require inertia/inertia-laravel

我们现在需要一个中间件，在我们的项目中赋予惯性力量。 在我们的终端中，输入：

 php artisan inertia:middleware

前往app/Http/目录，然后在Kernel.php文件中，在$middlewareGroups[]数组中添加以下行，在web[]数组中添加以下行：

 'web' => [ // ... \ App\Http\Middleware\HandleInertiaRequests :: class , ],

然后我们需要在前端识别我们的路由，因为我们没有使用刀片，而是使用 javascript 来呈现我们的前端。 因此，我们需要一个名为ziggy的特殊包来为我们处理这个问题。 我们通过 composer 安装它：

 composer require tightenco/ziggy

现在，在resources/views/目录中，让我们创建一个新的刀片文件app.blade.php ，这将是我们应用程序 UI 的入口点，因为我们将成为一个 SPA（单页应用程序）创造。 键入以下脚本：

 <!DOCTYPE html> <html lang= "{{ str_replace('_', '-', app()->getLocale()) }}" > <head> <meta charset= "utf-8" > <meta name= "viewport" content= "width=device-width, initial-scale=1" > <title inertia > {{ config('app.name', 'Laravel') }} </title> <!-- Scripts --> @routes @viteReactRefresh @vite('resources/js/app.jsx') @inertiaHead </head> <body> @inertia </body> </html>

注意@vite()和@viteReactRefresh 。 这些告诉我们的 Laravel 应用 Vite 正在编译我们的资产（CSS 和 JS 文件），并且我们将在前端使用 JSX。 请注意，我们不会从 app.blade.php 调用 css 文件。 但是，如果您愿意，您可以添加行@vite('resources/css/app.css') 。 理想情况下，这应该在我们将在本教程后面创建的resources/js/app.jsx文件中。

最后，让我们为欢迎页面创建路由。 前往routes/目录和web.php ，让我们添加以下行以使 Laravel 知道到我们的欢迎页面的路由：

 Route :: get ( '/' , function () { return inertia ( 'Welcome' ); } // other option (must add use Inertia\Inertia after namespace declaration at the top): /* Route::get('/', function () { return Inertia::render('Welcome'); } */

请注意注释掉的其他选项！ 他们中的任何一个都可以完美地工作。

设置前端

我们正在使用 npm 来安装我们的前端依赖项。 查看NodeJS 文档。 在您的终端中，键入以下命令：

 npm i @inertiajs/inertia @inertiajs/inertia-react jsconfig.json @inertiajs/progress

上面的命令将安装惯性前端依赖项、用于页面加载的惯性进度条以及标识我们依赖 javascript 呈现前端的项目的jsconfig.json文件。

有一个敏感问题。 我们还没有安装react-dom 。 为了不遇到错误，我们需要指定与我们已经安装的 reactjs 版本兼容的 react-dom 版本：

我们还需要用于 react 的 vite 插件：

 npm add @vitejs/plugin-react

前往resources/js/目录，在app.js文件中，在import "./bootstrap"行下方添加以下脚本，然后将文件重命名为app.jsx ：

 import " ../css/app.css " ; import React from " react " ; import { render } from " react-dom " ; import { createInertiaApp } from " @inertiajs/inertia-react " ; import { InertiaProgress } from " @inertiajs/progress " ; import { resolvePageComponent } from " laravel-vite-plugin/inertia-helpers " ; const appName = window . document . getElementsByTagName ( " title " )[ 0 ]?. innerText || " Laravel " ; createInertiaApp ({ title : ( title ) => ` ${ title } - ${ appName } ` , resolve : ( name ) => resolvePageComponent ( `./Pages/ ${ name } .jsx` , import . meta . glob ( " ./Pages/**/*.jsx " ) ), setup ({ el , App , props }) { return render (< App { ... props } />, el ); }, }); // you can specify any color of choice InertiaProgress . init ({ color : " #4B5563 " });

差不多好了！ 我们需要告诉 Vite 我们正在使用 react，并指定我们的入口点文件。 让我们去那里，修改并添加以下行：

 import { defineConfig } from " vite " ; import laravel from " laravel-vite-plugin " ; import react from " @vitejs/plugin-react " ; //this line export default defineConfig ({ plugins : [ laravel ({ input : " resources/js/app.jsx " , //this line }), react (), //this line ], });

最后，让我们创建欢迎页面。 让我们创建一个新文件夹Pages并在我们的resources/js/目录中添加一个Welcome.jsx文件：

 export default function Welcome () { return ( <> < div > Hello Inertia! </ div > </> ); }

然后使用以下命令启动您的 PHP 服务器：

然后在终端中添加一个选项卡并启动 Vite 开发服务器：

完毕！

结论

差不多就是这样！ 与以前的方法相比，它多了几个步骤。 但是，Vite 要好得多，并提供更好的开发人员体验。 您可以访问 Inertia 文档，该文档解释了有关客户端设置和服务器端设置的更多信息。



原文:

Ever since Laravel 9, a lot of new features and improvements have been included in Laravel. Furthermore, developer experience has been improved, especially with the introduction of Vite, a frontend asset bundler. Laravel was previously using webpack for years, and it did a very good job. Integrating React into Laravel 9 has changed a bit with Vite as the new frontend asset bundler. I wrote a previous article on how to integrate ReactJS into Laravel, but it works for Laravel 8.x and before, or anyone who is using webpack to compile the frontend. In this tutorial, we'll be covering how to do this with Laravel 9.

This tutorial is going to walk us through integrating ReactJS into Laravel from scratch. You could do this too using a starter kit.

Pre-requisites:

Setting up the backend

We can do this either using the Laravel installer or use composer. We are going to use the Laravel installer. Type the following command in your terminal:

laravel new laravel-react

Let's get into our new project:

We are going to need InertiaJS to help us contain these two stacks in one project. We're installing it using composer. Think of inertia as the glue to stick our frontend stack to our backend stack. In your terminal, type:

composer require inertia/inertia-laravel

We now need a middleware that gives inertia the power in our project. In our terminal, type:

php artisan inertia:middleware

Head to the app/Http/ directory, then in the Kernel.php file, add the follwing line in the $middlewareGroups[] array, add this line in the web[] array:

'web' => [ // ... \App\Http\Middleware\HandleInertiaRequests::class,
],

We then need our routes to be recognized in the frontend, since we're not using blade, but javascript to render our frontend. So, we need a special package called ziggy that handles that for us. We are installing it via composer:

composer require tightenco/ziggy

Now, in the resources/views/ directory, let's create a new blade file app.blade.php which will be our entry-point for our application's UI, since it's a SPA (single-page application) that we're going to be creating. Type the following script:


<!DOCTYPE html>
<html lang="{{ str_replace('_', '-', app()->getLocale()) }}"> <head> <meta charset="utf-8"> <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1"> <title inertia>{{ config('app.name', 'Laravel') }}</title> <!-- Scripts --> @routes @viteReactRefresh @vite('resources/js/app.jsx') @inertiaHead </head> <body> @inertia </body>
</html>

Notice the @vite() and @viteReactRefresh. These are telling our Laravel app that Vite is compiling our assets (CSS and JS files), and that we're going to be using JSX for our frontend. Note that we do not call the css file from the app.blade.php. But if you want to, you can just add the line @vite('resources/css/app.css'). Ideally, this should be in the resources/js/app.jsx file that we will create later in this tutorial.

Finally, let's create our route for our welcome page. Head to the routes/ directory and in the web.php, let's add the following line to make Laravel aware of the route to our welcome page:

Route::get('/', function () { return inertia('Welcome');
} // other option (must add use Inertia\Inertia after namespace declaration at the top): /*
Route::get('/', function () { return Inertia::render('Welcome');
}
*/

Note the other option commented out! Either one of them will work perfectly.

Setting up the frontend

We are using npm to install our frontend dependencies. The npm must be installed on your machine. Look at the NodeJS documentation. In your terminal, type the following command:

npm i @inertiajs/inertia @inertiajs/inertia-react jsconfig.json @inertiajs/progress

The above command will install inertia frontend dependencies, inertia progress bar for page loading and the jsconfig.json file that identifies our project that relies on javascript to render the frontend.

There's one sensitive issue. We have not yet installed react-dom. Inertia uses reactjs v17.0.2 as per the time of publishing this article. In order not to encounter errors, we need to specify the version of react-dom that will be compatible with our version of reactjs already installed:

We also need the vite plugin for react:

npm add @vitejs/plugin-react

Head to the resources/js/ directory, in the app.js file, add the following script below the import "./bootstrap" line, then rename the file to app.jsx:

import "../css/app.css"; import React from "react";
import { render } from "react-dom";
import { createInertiaApp } from "@inertiajs/inertia-react";
import { InertiaProgress } from "@inertiajs/progress";
import { resolvePageComponent } from "laravel-vite-plugin/inertia-helpers"; const appName = window.document.getElementsByTagName("title")[0]?.innerText || "Laravel"; createInertiaApp({ title: (title) => `${title} - ${appName}`, resolve: (name) => resolvePageComponent( `./Pages/${name}.jsx`, import.meta.glob("./Pages/**/*.jsx") ), setup({ el, App, props }) { return render(<App {...props} />, el); },
}); // you can specify any color of choice
InertiaProgress.init({ color: "#4B5563" });

Almost there! We need to tell Vite that we are using react, and specify our entry-point file. Laravel already installed for us a vite.config.js file. Let's head there, modify and add the following lines:

import { defineConfig } from "vite";
import laravel from "laravel-vite-plugin";
import react from "@vitejs/plugin-react"; //this line export default defineConfig({ plugins: [ laravel({ input: "resources/js/app.jsx", //this line }), react(), //this line ],
});

Finally, let's create our welcome page. Let's create a new folder Pages and add a Welcome.jsx file in our resources/js/ directory:

export default function Welcome () { return ( <> <div>Hello Inertia!</div> </> );
}

Then start your PHP server with the command:

Then add a tab in your terminal and start the Vite dev server:

Done!

Conclusion

And that's pretty much it! It's a few more steps compared to the previous way of doing it. But, Vite is much better and provides a better developer experience. You can visit the Inertia documentation that explains more on the client-side setup and server-side setup.
item.author: emokores256

item.title: 将 Javascript 项目转换为 Typescript | Converting a Javascript project to Typescript
item.description:
在过去的几年中，这一趋势已被多次记录，其中最重要的成就是 TypeScript 在著名的 Stack Overflow 开发人员调查中被评为 2020 年第二受欢迎的编程语言。

上述情况导致许多组织转向 TypeScript。 因此，大量用纯 Javascript 编写的遗留代码正在转换为 TypeScript。

最近，我参与了一个从 Javascript 到 TypeScript 的迁移项目，该项目是一个拥有数千名在线用户的成熟软件产品。 代码库庞大而复杂，在这个过程中重构了很多东西，但最终的结果是没有任何严重的缺陷。 这是因为我和我的同事遵循了非常严格的方法和计划。

因此，让我们对我们在离散步骤中遵循的过程进行一次演练。

过程

第 1 步：安装 TypeScript 编译器和加载器

您可以通过在项目的根目录中运行以下命令来执行此操作。

 npm install --save-dev typescript ts-loader

第 2 步：将所有依赖项替换为相应的 TypeScript 版本。

大多数通过包管理器（如 npm 或 yarn）公开可用的包也提供 TypeScript 版本。 大多数情况下，一些已知库的 TypeScript 版本都以@types为前缀。 例如，lodash 的 TypeScript 版本是@types/lodash包。

第三步：创建配置文件

正如官方 TypeScript 文档所述：

该文件应具有以下结构（假设项目的所有代码都位于 /src 目录下）：

 { "compilerOptions" : { "outDir" : "./dist/" , "noImplicitAny" : true , "sourceMap" : true , "module" : "es6" , "target" : "es5" , "jsx" : "react" , "allowJs" : true , "moduleResolution" : "node" }, "include" : [ "./src/**/*" ]
}

第 4 步：设置 Webpack

需要webpack.config.js文件来向 webpack 描述它将通过和处理源文件的方式。 下面，您可以看到一个 webpack 配置文件的示例，该文件将处理后的代码导出到 bundle.js 文件（输出键）。

 const path = require ( ' path ' ); module . exports = { entry : ' ./src/index.ts ' , module : { rules : [ { test : / \. tsx ? $/ , use : ' ts-loader ' , exclude : /node_modules/ , }, ], }, devtool : ' inline-source-map ' , resolve : { extensions : [ ' .tsx ' , ' .ts ' , ' .js ' ], }, output : { filename : ' bundle.js ' , path : path . resolve ( __dirname , ' dist ' ), }, };

第 5 步：将 .js 文件转换为 .ts 并将 .jsx 文件转换为 .tsx

现在是进行实际转换的时候了。 您只需将每个源文件的后缀/文件类型从 . 很简单吧？

第 6 步：声明接口和类型

如果您已正确完成上述所有步骤，则您已完成大部分转换过程。 此时，当您在根目录上运行经典的npm start命令时，项目应该可以正常运行。 但是，您没有利用 TypeScript 提供的任何优势，因为您还没有为对象和变量声明类型和接口。

现在，是时候为复杂对象编写您自己的类型和/或显式声明常量和变量的类型，您可以确定它们将获得的值类型。

 interface Options { color : string ; volume : number ; }

您可以在此链接上查看详细列表。

故障排除

如果您在此过程中遇到任何问题，请不要犹豫，查看以下链接：

以下 2 个实践将帮助您充分利用这项技术，并在错误到达生产环境之前避免错误。

  • 避免使用any和object类型

这些类型过于通用，对错误预防的价值非常有限。

  • 使您的自定义界面尽可能详细并使用嵌套

通过在接口级别使用嵌套，您也可以通过保持模块化架构来使应用程序类型安全。 随着代码库大小的增加，这种做法将得到回报，并且您需要重用您声明的类型。

结论

自出现以来，TypeScript 已经改变了 Web 开发世界中的许多事物。 似乎逐渐地，大多数“生活”在网络上的东西都将被重构为 TypeScript，以利用其巨大的功能。 我希望这篇文章能帮助你的项目更顺利地过渡到这个新时代。

如果您想查看更多此类内容，可以在Patreon上支持我！

感谢您的阅读，希望您喜欢！



原文:

Typescript is gaining more followers day-by-day. The type-safe cousin of Javascript is getting massively adopted and so does the projects and lines of TypeScript code written. This trend has been documented many times during the last years with the most important achievement being TypeScript voted as the 2nd most loved programming language for the year 2020 in the well-known Stack overflow developers survey.

The situation described above has lead many organizations to make a shift to TypeScript. Thus, large amounts of legacy code written in pure Javascript are being converted to TypeScript.

Recently, I took part in a migration project from Javascript to TypeScript for an established Software product with thousands of live users. The code base was large and complicated, so many things were refactored during the process but the final result was achieved without any serious drawbacks. This is because my colleagues and I, followed a very strict methodology and plan.

So let’s make a walkthrough over the process we followed in discrete steps.

The process

Step 1: Install TypeScript compiler and loader

You can do so by running the following command in the root directory of your project.

npm install --save-dev typescript ts-loader

Step 2: Replace any dependencies with the respective TypeScript version.

Most of the packages that are publicly available through package managers like npm or yarn, offer a TypeScript version as well. Most of times, TypeScript versions of some known libraries are prefixed with @types . For example, the TypeScript version of lodash is the @types/lodash package.

Step 3: Create a configuration file

As the official TypeScript documentation mentions:

TypeScript uses a file called tsconfig.json for managing your project’s options, such as which files you want to include, and what sorts of checking you want to perform.

The file should have the structure presented below (assuming that all the code of the project is located under the /src directory):

{ "compilerOptions": { "outDir": "./dist/", "noImplicitAny": true, "sourceMap": true, "module": "es6", "target": "es5", "jsx": "react", "allowJs": true, "moduleResolution": "node" }, "include": ["./src/**/*"]
}

Step 4: Setup Webpack

Webpack is a static module bundler that will handle the process of combining all the .ts and .tsx source files and export only the needed .js files. The webpack.config.js file is needed in order to describe to webpack the way in which it will go through and process the source files. Below, you can see an example of a webpack config file that exports the processed code to bundle.js file (output key).

const path = require('path'); module.exports = { entry: './src/index.ts', module: { rules: [ { test: /\.tsx?$/, use: 'ts-loader', exclude: /node_modules/, }, ], }, devtool: 'inline-source-map', resolve: { extensions: ['.tsx', '.ts', '.js'], }, output: { filename: 'bundle.js', path: path.resolve(__dirname, 'dist'), },
};

Step 5: Convert .js files to .ts and .jsx files to .tsx

Now it is time to do the actual conversion. You need to simply change the suffix / file type of every source file from .js to .ts and for the .jsx files, you need to change their suffix to .tsx (in case we have to to the conversion for a React project that uses JSX). Pretty straightforward uh ?

Step 6: Declare interfaces and types

If you have completed all the steps above correctly, you are done with most of the conversion process. In this point, the project should run correctly when you run the classic npm start command on the root directory. However, you are not taking any advantage that TypeScript offers, as you have not still declared types and interface for your objects and variables.

Now, it is time to write your own types for complex objects and / or explicitly declare types for constants and variables that you are sure about the kind of value that they will get.

interface Options { color: string; volume: number;
}

TypeScript offers a variety of basic types that you can use with boolean, string, number and enum among them. You can view an extensive list on this link.

Troubleshooting

In case you face any issue during the process, do not hesitate to have a look on the following links:

TypeScript good practices

The following 2 practices will help you get the most out of this technology and avoid errors before they reach to production environment.

  • Avoid the use of any and object types

These types are too generic and offer very limited value to error prevention.

  • Make your custom interfaces as detailed as possible and use nesting

By using nesting in the interface level, you are making the application type-safe by keeping a modular architecture too. This practice will pay off as the size of the code base increases and you need to reuse the types that you declared.

Conclusion

TypeScript has changed many things in the web development world since its appearance. It seems that gradually, most of anything that “lives” on web will be refactored to TypeScript in order to take advantage of its enormous capabilities. I hope that this article will help you achieve a smoother transition to this new era for your projects.

*That’s it and if you found this article helpful, please hit the ❤️, 🦄 button and share the article, so that others can find it easily :) *

If you want to see more of this content you can support me on Patreon!

Thanks for reading and hope you enjoyed!
item.author: kai_wenzel

item.title: JavaScript 中的 Object.freeze() | Object.freeze( ) in JavaScript
item.description:
湿婆亚亚尔

发表于在 JavaScript 世界中，我们已经知道类和对象的重要性。 在 Object 构造方法中，Object.freeze() 方法有助于冻结对象或数组。 冻结对象不允许更改对象的任何属性。 它返回传递的对象并且不创建冻结副本。

可以防止冻结的物体：

  • 向对象添加新属性。
  • 从对象中删除或更改现有属性。
  • 保留对象的可枚举性、可配置性、可写性和原型
  • 更改现有对象属性和原型的值。

语法是：

其中 obj 是必须被冻结的对象。

例子：

 Input : const obj = { name: 'initial_name'}; const newObj = Object.freeze(obj); newObj.name = 'new_name'; console.log(newObj.name); Output : "initial_name"

富兰克林马丁内斯—— 一旦暂停，connectaryal 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，connectaryal 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，connectaryal 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 connectaryal 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Shiva Aryal

Posted on Jul 5

In the JavaScript world we already know how the classes and objects are important. Among the Object constructor methods, the Object.freeze() method helps to freeze an object or arrays. Freezing an object does not allow to change any properties from the object. It returns the passed object and does not create a frozen copy.

Frozen object can be prevents from:

  • Adding new properties to the object.
  • Removing or altering existing properties from the object.
  • Preserves the enumerability, configurability, writability and the prototype of the object
  • Changing values of the existing object properties and prototype.

The syntax is:

where the obj is the object which has to be freezed.

Example:

Input : const obj = { name: 'initial_name'};
        const newObj = Object.freeze(obj);
        newObj.name = 'new_name';
        console.log(newObj.name);

Output : "initial_name"

Yehezkiel Gunawan - Jun 22

Franklin Martinez - Jul 1

Once suspended, connectaryal will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, connectaryal will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by connectaryal will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If connectaryal is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
