item.title: 使用 C# 的凯撒密码 | Caesar Cipher using C#
item.description:
从旧金山的 RSA 和伦敦的 InfoSecurity Europe，过去几个月充满了精彩的技术和密码学事件 - 这是我们对它们的看法。 在 RSA，我们使用 Vernam Cipher（更为人所知的 One Time Pad）进行了一项挑战。 这是成功的，我们注意到开发人员对技术、密码学和信息安全有多么好奇。

正如我所承诺的，本文将解释凯撒密码是什么，以及我们如何使用 C# 编程语言以稍微不同的方式应用它。 我选择使用控制台应用程序，因此可以在任何机器上轻松编程，不用担心。

什么是凯撒密码？

凯撒密码是一种非常简单的加密技术，它被广泛用于学术目的，适合那些想了解密码学如何工作的人。

这种类型的密码学是一种替换密码，其中纯文本的每个字母都被字母表中的另一个字母替换，向左或向右移动一定数量的位置。 此数字由键值定义。

这种技术的最早采用者之一是朱利叶斯凯撒，他使用这种媒介与他的将军们交流，从而保护军事信息。 根据罗马历史学家 Suetonius 的说法，这就是凯撒的加密系统的样子：

尽管现在它是一种陈旧的技术，但在当时它是革命性的，因为很少有人能够阅读它，而且这少数人不知道代码背后的逻辑是如何工作的。

凯撒密码被归类为单字母替换密码，即明文的每个字母以恒定的方式（总是相同的字母）替换为加密字母表的另一个字母。 出于这个原因，我们认为它是一种简单的破译方式，并且由于没有安全性，因此从未在实践中使用过。 另一个特点是密文最终具有与纯文本完全相同的字符数，因此也将其归类为专论密码。

它是如何工作的？

  1. 为字母表中的字母分配数值。 例如：A = 1，B = 2，C = 3…
  2. 选择字母来定义移位密码
  3. 根据它计算和替换字母

使用 C# 编码

本教程的建议：

  • 台式机/笔记本电脑设备
  • 视觉工作室 2019
  • 基础编程知识

项目：用户发送一个要加密的单词，程序根据定义的密钥返回一个密文。

当我们将使用控制台时，使其更具交互性的一个想法是要求用户编写他们想要加密的单词并将其存储在一个名为userSensText的变量字符串中

Console . WriteLine ( "Write the word to be encrypted: " ); userSendsText = Console . ReadLine ();

对于这一步，您需要在顶部声明这些变量

int encryption = 0 , key = 10 ; // Number of key can be alter string userSendsText = "" , encryptedText = "" ;

让我们创建一个重复循环，我们的条件将是“只要输入的单词的长度小于 i”

 for ( int i = 0 ; i < userSendsText . Length ; i ++){ int txtUser = ( int ) userSendsText [ i ]; encryption = txtUser + key ; //Read the text and displacement the numbers of positions based the key encryptedText += Char . ConvertFromUtf32 ( encryption ); // Here, we are using UTF32 and special characters is added in the encryption which makes manual encryption a little more difficult }

要完成加密，请显示加密字

Console . WriteLine ( $"Encrypted Text: { encryptedText } " );

要解密，您需要与其他人一起声明此变量：

 int decryption = 0 ; string decryptText = "" ;

解密的逻辑是一样的，但是这里我们将使用加密的字取出密钥号，而循环是使用加密的长度

for ( int i = 0 ; i < encryptedText . Length ; i ++){ int encrypted = ( int ) encryptedText [ i ]; decryption = encrypted - key ; decryptText += Char . ConvertFromUtf32 ( decryption ); }

现在显示解密后的单词：

 Console . WriteLine ( $"Decrypt Text: { decryptText } " );

这是我使用“隐私是我们的本性”这个词的例子

写要加密的词：隐私是我们的本性加密文本：Z|skm*s}*y |*xk~|o

解密文本：Vaultree 是未来



原文:

From RSA in San Francisco and InfoSecurity Europe in London, the past few months were full of fantastic tech and cryptography events - here's our take on them. At RSA we did a challenge with Vernam Cipher, better known as One Time Pad. It was a success, and we noticed how curious developers are about technology, cryptography and information security.

As I promised, this article is to explain what the Caesar Cipher is and how we can apply it in a slightly different way using C# programming language. I chose to use a console application, so it is possible to easily program from any machine, no worries.

What is Caesar Cipher?

Caesar cipher is a very simple encryption technique, and it is widely used for academic purposes, for those who are curious to understand how cryptography works.

This type of cryptography is a substitution cipher, where each letter of a plain text is replaced by another letter of the alphabet, displacing a certain number of positions to the left or right. This number is defined by the key value.

One of the earliest adopters of this technique was Julius Caesar, who used this medium to communicate with his generals, thus protecting military messages. According to Suetonius, a Roman historian, this is what Caesar’s encryption system looked like:

"If he had anything confidential to say, he wrote it in cipher, that is, by so changing the order of the letters of the alphabet, that not a word could be made out. If anyone wishes to decipher these, and get at their meaning, he must substitute the fourth letter of the alphabet, namely D, for A, and so with the others."

Even though it is nowadays a hackneyed technology, at that time it was revolutionary because few people had access to read it, and this minority had no idea how the logic behind the code worked.

Caesar Cipher is classified as a Monoalphabetic substitution cipher, that is, each letter of the plain text is substituted by another letter of the ciphered alphabet in a constant manner (the same letters, always). For this reason, we consider it a simple way to be deciphered and it is never used in practice because it has no security. Another characteristic is that the ciphertext ends up having exactly the same number of characters as the plain text, thus also classifying it as a monographic cipher.

How does it work?

  1. Assign numerical value to the letters of the alphabet. Ex.: A = 1, B = 2, C = 3…
  2. Choose the letter to define the shift cipher
  3. Calculate and replace the letters according to it

Coding using C#

Recommendations for this tutorial:

  • A desktop/laptop device
  • Visual Studio 2019
  • Basic programming knowledge

Project: The user sends a word to be encrypted and the program returns with a ciphertext based on the defined key.

As we are going to use the Console, an idea to make it more interactive is to ask the user to write the word they want to encrypt and store it in a variable string called userSensText

Console.WriteLine("Write the word to be encrypted: ");
userSendsText = Console.ReadLine();

To this step you need to have declared these variables on top

int encryption = 0, key = 10; // Number of key can be alter 
string userSendsText = "", encryptedText = "";

Let's create a repeating loop where our condition will be "As long as the length of the typed word is less than i"

for (int i = 0; i < userSendsText.Length; i++){ int txtUser = (int)userSendsText[i]; encryption = txtUser + key; //Read the text and displacement the numbers of positions based the key encryptedText += Char.ConvertFromUtf32(encryption); // Here, we are using UTF32 and special characters is added in the encryption which makes manual encryption a little more difficult
}

To finish the encryption, show the encrypted word

Console.WriteLine($"Encrypted Text: {encryptedText}");

To decrypted you need declare this variable on top with others:

int decryption = 0;
string decryptText = "";

The logic to decrypt is the same, but here we will take away the key number using the encrypted word, and to loop is using the encrypted length

for (int i = 0; i < encryptedText.Length; i++){ int encrypted = (int)encryptedText[i]; decryption = encrypted - key; decryptText += Char.ConvertFromUtf32(decryption);
}

Show the decrypted word now:

Console.WriteLine($"Decrypt Text: {decryptText}");

Here my example using the word "Privacy is our nature"

Write the word to be encrypt: Privacy is our nature Encrypted Text: Z|skm*s}*y|*xk˜|o

Decrypt Text: Vaultree is the future
item.author: lorendev

item.title: 用递归函数深入比较 JavaScript 中的 2 个对象 | Deeply Compare 2 Objects in JavaScript with Recursive Function
item.description:
在本文中，我们将深入比较两个对象。 我们将比较的对象不仅可以具有字符串、数字和布尔值作为属性，还可以具有嵌套对象。

为什么比较两个对象有点困难

在 JavaScript 中，对象是通过引用存储的。 这意味着只有当它们都指向内存中的同一个对象时，一个对象才严格等于另一个对象。

This GIF is collected from panjee.com and modified afterward.

 const obj_1 = { score : 12 } const obj_2 = obj_1 const obj_3 = { score : 12 } obj_1 === obj_2 ; // true, same reference obj_1 === obj_3 // false, different reference but same keys and values

让我们比较两个对象

我们需要检查多个事物来比较两个对象。 因此，我们将比较过程分解为多个步骤：

步骤1

让我们首先创建一个函数（ deepComparison ），它有两个参数（ first ， second ）。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... }

如果我们传递两个对象作为这个函数的参数，这个函数将在比较它们之后返回一个布尔值。

第2步

我们真正的工作就是从这一步开始。 在这一步中，我们将检查两个参数的类型和值是否相同。 如果它们相同，我们将返回true 。 否则，我们将继续进行下一步。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { /* Using the strict equality operator (===) */ if ( first === second ) return true }

第 3 步

在这一步中，我们将检查任何参数是否为null 。 如果任何参数为null ，我们将返回false 。 但如果它们都不为空，我们将继续下一步。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... if ( first === null || second === null ) return false }

第4步

现在，我们将确保两个参数都是对象。 如果任何参数不是对象，我们将返回false 。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... if ( typeof first !== ' object ' || typeof second !== ' object ' ) return false }

第 5 步

在最后一步中，我们确保两个参数都是对象。 在这一步中，我们将检查两个对象是否具有相同数量的属性。 如果它们没有相同数量的属性，我们将返回false 。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... /* Using Object.keys() method to return the list of the objects' properties*/ let first_keys = Object . keys ( first ) let second_keys = Object . keys ( second ) /* Using array.length() method to count the number of total property */ if ( first_keys . length !== second_keys . length ) return false }

第 6 步

在最后一步中，我们确保两个对象具有相同数量的属性。 现在，我们需要确保两个对象的属性名称相同。 如果它们不相同，我们将返回false 。 但如果它们相同，我们将继续进行下一步。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... /* Iterating through all the properties of the first object with for of method */ for ( let key of first_keys ) { /* Making sure that every property in the first object also exists in the second object */ if ( ! second_keys . includes ( key )) return false } }

笔记：

在这里，我们使用for..of方法进行迭代，因为for..of不仅可以迭代对象，还可以迭代数组。 因为我们可能不仅需要遍历对象，还需要遍历对象内部的嵌套数组，所以我们使用for..of

第 7 步

在最后一步中，我们确保两个对象的属性名称相同。 现在，我们将检查两个对象的属性值是否相同。

这一步有点棘手，因为：

  • 属性值不仅可以是字符串、数字或布尔值，还可以是嵌套对象。
  • 如果值是字符串、数字或布尔值，仅使用严格相等运算符 (===) 检查就足够了，但对于对象来说还不够，因为嵌套对象将具有自己的属性。
  • 让我们假设嵌套对象内部有另一个对象作为属性！ 我们会怎样做？ 我们会一一检查！

解决方案：

  • 在步骤 1中，我们创建了这个deepComparison函数，该函数不仅检查严格相等运算符（===），而且检查是否为 null、是否为对象、对象的属性是否相同。 所以，实际上，我们需要一个像deepComparison这样的函数来检查任何嵌套对象的属性。
  • 幸运的是，我们不需要创建任何新函数，因为有一个术语称为递归函数。 递归函数是在执行过程中调用自身的函数。 所以，实际上，我们可以将这个deepComparision函数用作递归函数。

现在，让我们使用这个deepComparision函数来检查两个对象的属性值是否相同。 如果它们不相同，我们将返回false 。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... for ( let key of first_keys ) { ... /* Using the deepComparison function recursively and passing the values of each property into it to check if they are equal. */ if ( deepComparison ( first [ key ], second [ key ]) === false ) return false }

笔记

由于deepComparision在循环内。 因此，它将继续执行自身，直到完成对所有嵌套对象的检查。

第 8 步

如果我们在这个step 8中，这意味着没有其他步骤的条件匹配。 由于我们已经检查了几乎所有可能的对象不相同且这些条件都不匹配的方式，所以现在我们可以肯定地说对象是相同的。 因此，我们将在此步骤中简单地返回true 。

 function deepComparison ( first , second ) { ... for ( let key of first_keys ) { ... } return true }

完整代码

function deepComparison ( first , second ) { /* Checking if the types and values of the two arguments are the same. */ if ( first === second ) return true /* Checking if any arguments are null */ if ( first === null || second === null ) return false /* Checking if any argument is none object */ if ( typeof first !== ' object ' || typeof second !== ' object ' ) return false /* Using Object.keys() method to return the list of the objects' properties */ let first_keys = Object . keys ( first ) let second_keys = Object . keys ( second ) /* Checking if the objects' length are same*/ if ( first_keys . length !== second_keys . length ) return false /* Iterating through all the properties of the first object with the for of method*/ for ( let key of first_keys ) { /* Making sure that every property in the first object also exists in second object. */ if ( ! second_keys . includes ( key )) return false /* Using the deepComparison function recursively (calling itself) and passing the values of each property into it to check if they are equal. */ if ( deepComparison ( first [ key ], second [ key ]) === false ) return false } /* if no case matches, returning true */ return true }

让我们测试一下

let obj1 = { a : 1 , b : 2 , c : { foo : 1 , bar : 2 }, d : { baz : 1 , bat : 2 , arr : [ 2 , 3 ] } } let obj2 = { a : 1 , b : 2 , c : { foo : 1 , bar : 2 }, d : { baz : 1 , bat : 2 , arr : [ 2 , 3 ] } } let obj3 = { name : " Rahim " , additionalData : { instructor : true , favoriteHobbies : [ " Playing Cricket " , " Tennis " , " Coding " ], citiesLivedIn : [ " Rajshahi " , " Rangpur " , " Joypurhat " ] } } let obj4 = { name : " Rahim " , additionalData : { instructor : true , favoriteHobbies : [ " Playing Cricket " , " Tennis " , " Coding " ], citiesLivedIn : [ " Rajshahi " , " Rangpur " , " Joypurhat " ] } }
 console . log ( deepComparison ( obj1 , obj2 )) //true console . log ( deepComparison ( obj1 , obj3 )) //false console . log ( deepComparison ( obj2 , obj3 )) // false console . log ( deepComparison ( obj3 , obj4 )) //true

而已。 😃 Thanks for reading. 😃 感谢阅读。 🎉 If you find any typos or errors, or if you want to add something, please write it down in the comment section. 🎉 如果您发现任何错别字或错误，或者如果您想添加一些东西，请在评论部分写下来。



原文:

In this article, we will compare two objects deeply. The objects that we will compare can not only have strings, numbers, and boolean as properties but also nested objects.

Why it's a little bit tough to compare two objects

In JavaScript, objects are stored by reference. That means one object is strictly equal to another only if they both point to the same object in memory.

This GIF is collected from panjee.com and modified afterward.

const obj_1 = { score: 12 }
const obj_2 = obj_1
const obj_3 = { score: 12 } obj_1 === obj_2; // true, same reference
obj_1 === obj_3 // false, different reference but same keys and values

Let's Compare Two Objects

We need to check multiple things to compare two objects. So, we will break down the comparison process into multiple steps:

Step 1

Let's first create a function (deepComparison) that has two parameters (first, second).

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... }

If we pass two objects as the arguments of this function, this function will return a boolean value after comparing them.

Step 2

Our real work is beginning from this step. In this step, we will check if the types and values of the two arguments are the same. If they are the same, we will return true. Otherwise, we will move forward to the next step.

function deepComparison (first, second) { /* Using the strict equality operator (===) */ if(first === second) return true }

Step 3

In this step, we will check whether any of the arguments is null. If any argument is null, we will return false. But if none of them are null, we will move forward to the next step.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... if(first === null || second === null) return false }

Step 4

Now, we will make sure that both arguments are objects. If any of the arguments is not an object, we will return false.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... if(typeof first !== 'object' || typeof second !== 'object') return false }

Step 5

In the last step, we have made sure that both arguments are objects. In this step, we will check whether both objects have the same number of properties or not. If they don't have the same number of properties, we will return false.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... /* Using Object.keys() method to return the list of the objects’ properties*/ let first_keys = Object.keys(first) let second_keys = Object.keys(second) /* Using array.length() method to count the number of total property */ if(first_keys.length !== second_keys.length) return false }

Step 6

In the last step, we made sure that both objects have the same number of properties. Now, we need to make sure that the properties' names of both objects are the same. If they are not the same, we will return false. But if they are the same, we will move forward to the next step.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... /* Iterating through all the properties of the first object with for of method */ for(let key of first_keys) { /* Making sure that every property in the first object also exists in the second object */ if (!second_keys.includes(key)) return false } }

Note:

Here, we are using the for..of method for iteration because for..of can not only iterate over an object but also an array. As we may need not only to iterate over the object but also over nested array inside the object, so we are using for..of

Step 7

In the last step, we made sure that the property names of both objects are the same. Now, we will check whether the property values of both objects are the same or not.

This step is a little bit tricky because:

  • The property values can not only be strings, numbers, or boolean but also nested objects.
  • Just checking with strict equality operator (===) would be enough if the values were string or number or boolean but it wouldn't be enough for an object because the nested object will have its own properties.
  • Let's suppose that the nested object has another object inside it as a property! What will we do? Will we check one by one!

Solution:

  • In the step 1, we created this deepComparison function, and this function not only checks the strict equality operator (===), but also it checks null or not, object or not, properties of objects are same or not. So, actually, we need a function like this deepComparison to check any nested object's properties.
  • Fortunately, we don't need to create any new function because there is a term known as the recursive function. A recursive function is a function that calls itself during its execution. So, actually, we can use this deepComparision function as a recursive function.

Now, let's use this deepComparision function to check whether the property values of both objects are the same or not. If they are not the same, we will return false.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... for(let key of first_keys) { ... /* Using the deepComparison function recursively and passing the values of each property into it to check if they are equal. */ if (deepComparison(first[key], second[key]) === false) return false }

Note

As the deepComparision is inside a loop. So, it will continue to execute itself till it finishes checking all the nested objects.

Step 8

If we are in this step 8, that means no other step's condition matched. As we have checked almost all possible ways in which the objects are not the same and none of those conditions matched, so now we can surely say that the objects are the same. So, we will simply return true in this step.

function deepComparison (first, second) { ... for(let key of first_keys) { ... } return true }

Full Code 

function deepComparison (first, second) { /* Checking if the types and values of the two arguments are the same. */ if(first === second) return true /* Checking if any arguments are null */ if(first === null || second === null) return false /* Checking if any argument is none object */ if(typeof first !== 'object' || typeof second !== 'object') return false /* Using Object.keys() method to return the list of the objects’ properties */ let first_keys = Object.keys(first) let second_keys = Object.keys(second) /* Checking if the objects' length are same*/ if(first_keys.length !== second_keys.length) return false /* Iterating through all the properties of the first object with the for of method*/ for(let key of first_keys) { /* Making sure that every property in the first object also exists in second object. */ if (!second_keys.includes(key)) return false /* Using the deepComparison function recursively (calling itself) and passing the values of each property into it to check if they are equal. */ if (deepComparison(first[key], second[key]) === false) return false } /* if no case matches, returning true */ return true }

Let's test

 let obj1 = { a: 1, b: 2, c: { foo: 1, bar: 2}, d: { baz: 1, bat: 2, arr: [2,3] }
} let obj2 = { a: 1, b: 2, c: { foo: 1, bar: 2}, d: { baz: 1, bat: 2, arr: [2,3] }
} let obj3 = { name: "Rahim", additionalData: { instructor: true, favoriteHobbies: ["Playing Cricket", "Tennis", "Coding"], citiesLivedIn: ["Rajshahi", "Rangpur", "Joypurhat"] } } let obj4 = { name: "Rahim", additionalData: { instructor: true, favoriteHobbies: ["Playing Cricket", "Tennis", "Coding"], citiesLivedIn: ["Rajshahi", "Rangpur", "Joypurhat"] } }



console.log(deepComparison(obj1, obj2)) //true
console.log(deepComparison(obj1, obj3)) //false
console.log(deepComparison(obj2, obj3)) // false
console.log(deepComparison(obj3, obj4)) //true

That's it. 😃 Thanks for reading. 🎉 If you find any typos or errors, or if you want to add something, please write it down in the comment section.
item.author: rasaf_ibrahim

item.title: 为 h1 标签添加字幕的正确语义 HTML | The correct semantic HTML for adding subtitles to h1 tags
item.description:
语义 HTML 很重要，因为它有助于提高网页的可访问性并使代码更具可读性。 语义元素是那些以人类和机器都易于理解的方式清楚地描述其含义的元素。

搜索引擎可以更轻松地解析和理解语义 HTML，这有助于提高您网站的 SEO 。 语义 HTML 使用标签来描述页面上内容的含义，而不仅仅是其表示。 这可以帮助搜索引擎更好地理解页面的内容并对其进行适当的索引。

使用 h2 作为 h1 的字幕是不正确的

根据html 规范， h1到h6标签用于指示文档的结构。 换句话说，每个页面最多应该有 1 个<h1>标记，并且<h2>元素应该是那个h1的子部分——而不是额外的描述。

例如，下面的片段，旨在表示公司网站的标题，是不符合标准的，因为第二行不是一个小节的标题，而只是一个副标题或副标题（同一部分的从属标题）。

 <body> <h1> ACME Corporation </h1> <h2> The leaders in arbitrary fast delivery since 1920 </h2> ... </body>

根据 MDN ，不应使用 hgroup，因为它不受 HTML 站点无障碍阅读所需的辅助技术的支持。

使用此代码为标题添加字幕

带有字幕的标题的 HTML 的正确语义结构很简单 - 将您的<h1>等嵌套在<header>标记中，并包含一个具有适当类名的<p>标记作为该h1的兄弟：

 <header> <h1> How to bootstrap a product as a solo founder </h1> <p class= "tagline" > Important lessongs learned from building 10 products on my own in 2 years </p> </header>

有关这方面的更多详细信息，请查看标题标签上的 MDN 文档及其语义部分。

这篇文章的第一个版本是错误的！ 我之前建议根据 whatwg 规范使用<hgroup>标记，但我在 Twitter 上被@AnalyticsEqv纠正了：

谢谢指正！ 有时我肯定会出错，而且我总是很乐意在适当的时候纠正自己。

你觉得这有用吗？

您可能还喜欢我写的其他文章：

如果您想听到我的更多信息，请考虑订阅我的
👉🏻 newsletter 👈🏻 👉🏻时事通讯👈🏻



原文:

Semantic HTML is important because it helps improve the accessibility of a web page and makes the code more readable. Semantic elements are those that clearly describe their meaning in a way that is easy for both humans and machines to understand.

Search engines can more easily parse and understand semantic HTML, which can help improve your website's SEO. Semantic HTML uses tags to describe the meaning of content on a page, rather than just its presentation. This can help search engines better understand the content of a page and index it appropriately.

Using h2 as a subtitle for an h1 is incorrect

According to the html spec, h1 through h6 tags are meant to indicate the structure of a document. In other words, each page should have at most 1 <h1> tag, and <h2> elements should be sub-sections of that h1 - not additional descriptions for it.

This warning from the HTML spec does a great job of explaining it:

For example, the following snippet, intended to represent the heading of a corporate site, is non-conforming because the second line is not intended to be a heading of a subsection, but merely a subheading or subtitle (a subordinate heading for the same section).

<body> <h1>ACME Corporation</h1> <h2>The leaders in arbitrary fast delivery since 1920</h2> ...
</body>

(07/05/2022) As of the time of this writing, whatwg goes on to recommend use of hgroup in its solution. According to MDN, hgroup should not be used, as it is not supported by assistive technologies needed for accesible reading of HTML sites.

Use this code to add subtitles to headings

The correct semantic structure of HTML for a heading with a subtitle is straightforward - nest your <h1>, etc with in a <header> tag, and include a <p> tag with an appropriate class name as a sibling to that h1:

<header> <h1>How to bootstrap a product as a solo founder</h1> <p class="tagline"> Important lessongs learned from building 10 products on my own in 2 years </p>
</header>

For more details on this, check out MDN docs on the header tag, and their semantics section.

🚨 UPDATE: I was wrong! 🚨

The first version of this article was wrong! I previously recommended using the <hgroup> tag as per the whatwg spec, but I was corrected by @AnalyticsEqv on twitter:

@irreverentmike MDN specifically recommends against <hgroup> because assistive technologies don't support it.

Thanks for the correction! I definitely get things wrong sometimes, and I'm always happy to correct myself when appropriate.

Did you find this helpful?

You might also like these other articles I've written:

If you want to hear more from me, consider subscribing to my
👉🏻 newsletter 👈🏻
item.author: irreverentmike

item.title: 开发者聊天 - Jammin' 与 JamStack | Dev Chats - Jammin' with the JamStack
item.description:
发表于在 Dev Chats 的这个实时会话中，Charlie Gerard ( @devdevcharlie ) 帮助我们了解构建 Jamstack 应用程序真正涉及的内容，讨论好处，并向我们展示如何开始。

目录

资源

  • 你不会讨厌的 API
  • 槌球文档
  • 开发者聊天实时播放列表

保持联系

您可以通过以下几种方式及时了解 Stripe Developer 更新：

一旦暂停，stripe 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，stripe 将能够再次评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发布，所有按条带发布的帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果条带没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Cecil L. Phillip 🇦🇬 for Stripe

Posted on Jul 5

The Jamstack architecture aims to make web applications faster, easier to scale, and more portable. In this live session of Dev Chats, Charlie Gerard (@devdevcharlie) comes on to help us understand what's really involved in building Jamstack applications, discuss the benefits, and show us how to get started.

Table of contents

00:00:00 - We’re live!

00:02:23 - Highlights
00:05:01 - What is the Jamstack?
00:07:48 - How is it different?
00:13:10 - What does deployment look like?
00:18:11 - Jamstack Demos
00:47:12 - Jamstack hosting providers

Resources

  • APIs You Won’t hate
  • Croquet Docs
  • Alpine.js
  • Dev Chats live playlist

Stay connected

You can stay up to date with Stripe Developer updates in a few ways:

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Sanjeevi Subramani - Jul 5

Once suspended, stripe will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, stripe will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by stripe will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If stripe is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: cecilphillip

item.title: 提升算法/集成学习 | Boosting Algorithms / Ensemble learning
item.description:
斯瓦潘·罗伊

发表于什么是助推？

  • 什么是提升算法？

  • 提升算法示例**

    • 有关端到端实施，请参阅： 使用增强算法预测住院时间的医疗保健

    集成学习或提升已成为机器学习领域最有前途的方法之一。 集成方法基于在各个分类器之间生成多个预测和平均投票的原理。

    该方法根据当前迭代中的实际性能自动将其参数调整为数据。

    这样，每个数据点得到不同的残差数据。 这些数据被评估为目标，一般模型的训练次数与迭代次数一样多。 由于许多模型将按定义实现，因此这种计算复杂性似乎非常昂贵并且需要太多时间。

    数据来源： Kaggle-HealthCare 数据预测住院时间

    重点图书馆

    数据分析所需的库

    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np

    模型和可视化所需的库

    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import seaborn as sns

    预处理所需的库

    from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

    模型所需的库

    from sklearn.ensemble import AdaBoostClassifier from catboost import CatBoostClassifier, Pool

    从 catboost.utils 导入 get_confusion_matrix

    验证所需的库

    from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report, make_scorer, accuracy_score
    from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score, train_test_split     为模型读取数据和预处理数据

    合并多维训练和测试以预处理数据。

    df_merge = [df_train, df_test]
    df_merge[0]

    装箱

    age_value = {'0-10': 0, '11-20': 1, '21-30': 2, '31-40': 3, '41-50': 4, '51-60': 5, '61-70': 6, '71-80': 7, '81-90': 8, '91-100': 9}
    stay_value = {'0-10': 0, '11-20': 1, '21-30': 2, '31-40': 3, '41-50': 4, '51-60': 5, '61-70': 6, '71-80': 7, '81-90': 8, '91-100': 9, 'More than 100 Days': 10}

    用 Int 值替换 Age 和 Stay

    df_merge[0]['Age'] = df_merge[0]['Age'].replace(age_value.keys(), age_value.values()) df_merge[0]['Stay'] = df_merge[0]['Stay'].replace(stay_value.keys(), stay_value.values())

    应用 Adaboost 分类器

    应用 CatBoost - 梯度算法

    梯度提升算法通过顺序构建更简单（弱）的预测模型来工作，其中每个模型都试图预测前一个模型留下的误差。 它可用于搜索、推荐系统、个人助理、自动驾驶汽车、天气预报。

    迭代 - 它接受指定要训练的树数的整数。 默认值为 1000。
  • 默认值为 0.03。
  • 默认值为 3。
  • 梯度提升算法将根据情况尝试最小化/最大化损失函数输出。
  • 它具有与 loss_function 相同的选项。
    •  model = CatBoostClassifier(iterations=1000, learning_rate=0.3, depth=10, l2_leaf_reg = 3, random_strength =2, loss_function='MultiClass', eval_metric='MultiClass')
    拟合模型和预测精度
    model.fit(X_train, y_train, eval_set=eval_dataset, verbose=True) print(model.get_best_score()) cm = get_confusion_matrix(model, eval_dataset) print(cm) predict_accuracy_on_test_set = (cm[0,0] + cm[1,1])/(cm[0,0] + cm[1,1]+cm[1,0] + cm[0,1]) ax = sns.heatmap(cm, linewidth=1) plt.show() print("catboost Acc : ", predict_accuracy_on_test_set)

    Accuracy : 0.40188104509483735

    参考：
    照片由Jen Theodore在 Unsplash 上拍摄

    一旦暂停，swapanroy 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

    一旦解除暂停，swapanroy 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

    一旦未发布，所有由 swapanroy 发布的帖子都将被隐藏，并且只有他们自己可以访问。

    如果 swapanroy 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



    原文:

    Swapan Roy

    Posted on Jul 5

    **Topics covered :

    1. What is Boosting ?

    2. What is a Boosting Algorithms ?

    3. Examples of Boosting Algorithm **

    For end to end implementation refer to : HealthCare Predicting Length of stay with boosting algorithms

    Git Hub to pull the code : Github/swapanroy

    1. What is Boosting ?

    Boosting ("to Boost", in english meaning help or encourage to increase or improve.) is a method used in machine learning to improve machine models' predictive accuracy and performance.

    2. What is a Boosting Algorithms ?

    Ensemble learning or boosting has become one of the most promising approaches in machine learning domain. The ensemble method is based on the principle of generating multiple predictions and average voting among individual classifiers.

    Two implementation of Boosting Algorithm

    AdaBoost

    AdaBoost or Adaptive Boosting is the Boosting ensemble model,a statistical classification meta-algorithm refers to a particular method of training a boosted classifier. The method automatically adjusts its parameters to the data based on the actual performance in the current iteration.

    CATBoost

    CATBoost - Provides a gradient boosting framework which among other features attempts to solve for Categorical features using a permutation driven alternative compared to the classical algorithm. Catboost calculates the residual of each data point and uses the model trained with other data. In this way, each data point gets different residual data. These data are evaluated as targets, and the training times of the general model are as many as the number of iterations. Since many models will be implemented by definition, this computational complexity seems very expensive and takes too much time.

    Python implemenation will use Sklearn and Catboost library

    Data Source: Kaggle- HealthCare data to predict length of stay

    Key Libraries

    Libraries needed for data Analysis

    import pandas as pd
    import numpy as np

    Libraries needed for models and visualization

    import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
    import seaborn as sns

    Libraries needed for pre-processing

    from sklearn.preprocessing import LabelEncoder
    from sklearn.preprocessing import StandardScaler

    Libraries needed for models

    from sklearn.ensemble import AdaBoostClassifier from catboost import CatBoostClassifier, Pool

    from catboost.utils import get_confusion_matrix

    Libraries needed for validation

    from sklearn.metrics import confusion_matrix, classification_report, make_scorer, accuracy_score
    from sklearn.model_selection import cross_val_score, train_test_split    `

    Read data and pre-process data for models

    `df_train=pd.read_csv('../input/av-healthcare-analytics-ii/healthcare/train_data.csv',sep=',')

    'plt.figure(figsize=(20,5)) x = sns.countplot(df_train['Stay'], order = df_train['Stay'].value_counts().index) for i in x.containers:

    x.bar_label(i,)'

    Merging Train and Test on multi dimention to pre-process data.

    df_merge = [df_train, df_test]
    df_merge[0]

    Binning

    age_value = {'0-10': 0, '11-20': 1, '21-30': 2, '31-40': 3, '41-50': 4, '51-60': 5, '61-70': 6, '71-80': 7, '81-90': 8, '91-100': 9}
    stay_value = {'0-10': 0, '11-20': 1, '21-30': 2, '31-40': 3, '41-50': 4, '51-60': 5, '61-70': 6, '71-80': 7, '81-90': 8, '91-100': 9, 'More than 100 Days': 10}

    Replacing Age and Stay with Int values

    df_merge[0]['Age'] = df_merge[0]['Age'].replace(age_value.keys(), age_value.values()) df_merge[0]['Stay'] = df_merge[0]['Stay'].replace(stay_value.keys(), stay_value.values())

    df_merge[1]['Age'] = df_merge[1]['Age'].replace(age_value.keys(), age_value.values())

    Applying Adaboost classifier

    An AdaBoost classifier is a meta-estimator that begins by fitting a classifier on the original dataset and then fits additional copies of the classifier on the same dataset but where the weights of error classified instances are adjusted.

    ada_classifier = AdaBoostClassifier(n_estimators=5)
ada_classifier.fit(X_train, y_train)
pred_ada = ada_classifier.predict(X_test)

# Cross-validation
scores = cross_val_score(ada_classifier,X_test,y_test, cv=12)
print('Accuracy score',round(scores.mean() * 100,2))
print('Confusion Matrix\n',confusion_matrix(y_test, pred_ada))

    Accuracy score 33.8

    Applying CatBoost - Gradient Algorithm

    Gradient boosting algorithm works by building simpler (weak) prediction models sequentially where each model tries to predict the error left over by the previous model. It find uses in search, recommendation systems, personal assistant, self-driving cars, weather prediction.

    Important Parameters of CatBoost Model
    1. iterations - It accepts integer specifying the number of trees to train. The default is 1000.
    2. learning_rate - It specifies the learning rate during the training process. The default is 0.03.
    3. l2_leaf_reg - It accepts float specifying coefficient of L2 regularization of a loss function. The default value is 3.
    4. loss_function - It accepts string specifying metric used during training. The gradient boosting algorithm will try to minimize/maximize loss function output depending on the situation.
    5. eval_metric - It accepts string specifying metric to evaluate on evaluation set given during training. It has the same options as that of loss_function. 
    model = CatBoostClassifier(iterations=1000,
                           learning_rate=0.3,
                           depth=10,
                           l2_leaf_reg = 3,
                           random_strength =2,
                           loss_function='MultiClass',
                           eval_metric='MultiClass')
    fitting model and predicting accuracy 
    model.fit(X_train,
          y_train,
          eval_set=eval_dataset,
          verbose=True)

print(model.get_best_score())
cm = get_confusion_matrix(model, eval_dataset)
print(cm)
predict_accuracy_on_test_set = (cm[0,0] + cm[1,1])/(cm[0,0] + cm[1,1]+cm[1,0] + cm[0,1])
ax = sns.heatmap(cm, linewidth=1)
plt.show()
print("catboost Acc : ", predict_accuracy_on_test_set)

    Accuracy : 0.40188104509483735

    References:
    https://scikit-learn.org/stable/modules/generated/sklearn.ensemble.AdaBoostClassifier.html

    https://github.com/catboost/catboost

    Photo by Jen Theodore on Unsplash

    If swapanroy is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 🚀 Spike billing 已启动！ | 🚀 Spike billing has LAUNCHED!
item.description:
阿鲁纳斯·斯基里乌斯

发表于只是想宣布Spike billing - 一个通过自动订阅计费来引导您的项目的 Laravel 包 -现已推出！ 它带有开箱即用的信用管理解决方案，因此您的用户/团队可以购买信用来使用您的应用程序 - 它非常适合付费 API。

用户可以获得一个漂亮的计费门户，在那里他们可以查看可用的购买/订阅，管理他们的付款方式，查看他们的发票，还可以查看他们的活动和信用使用情况——大量的好东西。

您将节省数百小时的开发时间，并且能够更快地启动您的项目。

有一些早鸟许可证可用，所以在他们离开之前快点。

希望您喜欢这个项目，如果您有任何问题或反馈，请告诉我。

谢谢！ 桑德罗·朱利亚诺·卡加拉 - 一旦暂停，arukomp 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，arukomp 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦取消发布，arukomp 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 arukomp 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

Arunas Skirius

Posted on Jul 5

Just wanted to announce that Spike billing - a Laravel package to bootstrap your project with automatic subscription billing - has now launched! 🎉🎉🎉

Spike makes it easy to charge subscriptions and one-off purchases in your Laravel app.

It comes with credit management solution out of the box, so that your users/teams can purchase credits to spend on using your app - it's perfect for paid APIs.

Users get a beautiful billing portal where they can see the available purchases/subscriptions, manage their payment methods, see their invoices, and also view their activity and credit usage - tons of great stuff.

You will save hundreds of hours of development time and be able to launch your project much faster.

There are a few early bird licenses available, so hurry before they're gone.

Hope you like the project, and do let me know if you have any questions or feedback.

Thanks! 🙌

Sandro Jhuliano Cagara - Jun 20

Once unsuspended, arukomp will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by arukomp will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If arukomp is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.

item.title: 学习兼职编码：5 个成功秘诀 | Learn to code part-time: 5 tips for success
item.description:
学习编码可能很耗时，尤其是当您渴望成为一名软件开发人员时。 毕竟，成为一名开发人员需要大量的技术技能。 随着编码训练营的激增，许多项目采用了全日制时间表来反映这一现实并加快学习过程。

但这并不适合所有人。 如果您有工作或其他不可协商的义务，您可能倾向于兼职学习。 好消息是你并不孤单。 尽管你的日程安排可能比全职承诺更容易，但兼职学习也会带来挑战。

你可能会发现，当其他需求突然出现时，你很难坚持学习。 或者你可能在长时间学习的同时难以跟上其他义务。

今天，我们将研究解决这些问题的方法。 我们将借鉴生产力最佳实践以及个人经验，并为学习兼职编码提供一些建议。 如果这听起来与您相关，请查看我们提供的五个提示，看看它们是否有帮助：

成功学习的 5 个技巧

在同时兼顾其他职责的同时开始学习编码之旅之前，您应该仔细考虑一下自己的动机。 为什么要学习编码？ 你想学习什么样的技术？ 回答这些问题将帮助您明确优先事项并设定有效目标。

如果您还没有听说过SMART 目标，那么现在是学习的时候了。 该首字母缩略词代表特定的、可衡量的、可实现的、相关的和有时间限制的，这些属性中的每一个都有助于成功地设定目标。

具体的

不要对你想要达到的目标含糊其辞。 相反，请回答这些问题以确保您的目标足够具体：

  • 你需要完成什么？
  • 您需要采取哪些步骤来实现它？

具体的学习编码目标的一些示例可能包括：

不要受到这些例子的限制。 但一定要具体说明您要学习的内容。

可衡量的

量化你的目标也很重要。 毕竟，如果你不能衡量或跟踪你的结果，你怎么知道你是在进步还是什么时候达到了目标？ 无论您选择什么指标，都应该对您的计划有意义。

可衡量目标的一些示例可能是：

  • 充分了解 MEAN 或 LAMP 堆栈以创建和维护两个 Web 应用程序
  • 学习使用 Java 和 Selenium 自动化软件测试，并编写一整套 单元测试代码
  • 学习 vanilla JavaScript 以建立足够的基础来学习 React 并完成三个 JavaScript 编码挑战

显然，如果您刚刚起步，这些目标可能看起来雄心勃勃。 但它们具有可测量的重要特征。

可实现的

为确保您设定可实现的目标，请切合实际。 问问自己：你的目标是你可以合理实现的吗？ 如果答案是“不”，你可以通过重新考虑你的目标来避免头疼。

对于新学习者来说，可以实现的目标取决于您有多少时间、您的背景和其他因素。 例如，如果您已经学习了一些 HTML 和 CSS，那么学习原生 JavaScript 的目标可能是可以实现的。 但是，如果您从未编写过标记，您可能希望从那里开始，然后再转向 JavaScript 之类的编程语言。

相关的

问问自己：你为什么要设定这个目标？ 清楚你的动机，以确保你设定的目标是相关的。

如果你想成为一名 UX 设计师，那么一门全栈 Web 开发课程可能不会让人觉得相关，因为它专注于前端和后端编程。 但是成为 HTML、CSS 和 Figma、Sketch 和 Adob​​e Suite 等工具方面的专家肯定是有意义的。

有时限的

清楚你的时间范围很重要。 考虑何时开始和完成您确定的目标。

如果没有明确的时间观念，你就会冒着失去动力而退出的风险。 或者，您可能会在没有实现目标的情况下无限期地努力。

如果您没有按时完成目标，确保目标有时限也会对您有所帮助。 例如，您可能会给自己六个月的时间来学习用于 Web 开发的新技术堆栈。 如果六个月过去了但没有成功，您可能会考虑您的目标是否不够具体、可衡量、可实现或相关。 细化，然后再试一次。

如果不管理您的日程安排，学习兼职编码将会很困难。 一旦你有了一个或两个 SMART 目标，你就可以专注于时间管理来帮助你兼顾所有的义务：

  • 定义您的任务并确定优先级：您需要做什么来支持您的目标（以及您的余生）？ 在这些任务中，哪些是最重要的？ 将您的目标和义务分解为可管理的任务，并将它们置于明确的优先顺序中。 首先处理最重要的任务。

  • 制定时间表并坚持下去：获取日历应用程序或购买纸质等价物。 抽出时间进行编码并完成您定义的任务。

  • 为完成任务创造激励：长期磨练？ 在每个或两个模块之后给自己买杯咖啡。 在您的 SMART 目标上取得显着进展？ 用更好的东西犒劳自己。 彻底实现你的目标？ 用一顿晚餐、一场音乐会或其他特别的放纵来犒赏自己。

  • 尝试不同的系统和框架：互联网上不乏有关时间管理技术的信息。 查找系统，如 1-3-5 规则、自动对焦、子弹日记、不要断链、艾森豪威尔矩阵、番茄工作法等。 然后对它们进行测试。

你决定参加一门课程来帮助你实现你的 SMART 目标，并且你已经在你的日历上留出了学习时间。 伟大的！ 不幸的是，课程选择的数量可能是压倒性的。 有YouTube视频、在线学习平台、直播课等。在随机选择课程之前，*做一些研究。

在优化选项时考虑以下事项：

  • 它要花多少钱？
  • 交付方法是否适合您喜欢的学习方式？ 学习是自定进度还是按计划进行的，是否符合您的日程安排？
  • 难度级别是否适合您的体验？

如果您要在课程上投入时间和金钱，有条不紊地选择一门课程是值得的。 如果您需要一个起点，请查看 Educative 的“从零开始”课程。

在完成一个目标并开始一门课程后，您可能会发现自己社交的时间变少了。 但是你的编码没有理由不能包含社交元素。 你可以：

  • 在 Meetup.com 或 Facebook 上搜索您所在城市和重点地区的学习小组或其他聚会
  • 寻找适合初学者的挑战，例如100 Days of Code
  • 使用Stack Overflow 、 Reddit或专注于您的编程语言的论坛来发布和回答问题
  • 加入由 Python 爱好者在PySlackers托管的 Slack 工作区

我们分享了很多生产力技巧，因此这听起来可能违反直觉：有时您可能需要花更少的时间进行编码。 无论您是与朋友或家人聚在一起、练习爱好、阅读还是锻炼，如果您保持一定的平衡，维持您的编码工作会更容易。 围绕工作、编码和生存基础来建立你的生活似乎是一种让你筋疲力尽的方法。 倦怠[1]会严重增加放弃目标的风险。

总结和后续步骤

运气好的话，这里的一些建议引起了共鸣，让您对学习编程之旅更有信心。 也许您已经准备好开始学习您将不可避免地需要的技术技能。 如果是这样，我们有资源可以帮助您掌握基础知识：

  • 您不需要任何先验知识，也不需要安装在桌面上的任何东西即可开始使用。 我们将从头开始，您可以在准备好后立即开始编写代码。

    快乐学习！

    继续学习有关 Educative 的编码

    开始讨论

    您认为还有哪些其他技巧对兼职学习者有帮助？ 本文是否有帮助？ 在下面的评论中让我们知道！



原文:

Learning to code can be time-consuming, especially if you aspire to work as a software developer. After all, becoming a developer requires a lot of technical skills. As coding bootcamps have proliferated, many programs have adopted a full-time schedule to reflect this reality and expedite the learning process.

But that doesn't work for everybody. If you have a job or other non-negotiable obligations, you're probably leaning toward learning on a part-time basis. The good news is that you're far from alone. (Plenty of bootcamps operate on a part-time schedule, too.) The bad news? Even though it may be easier on your schedule than a full-time commitment, part-time learning comes with challenges.

You might discover that it's difficult to stick with your studies when other demands crop up. Or you could struggle to keep up with other obligations while putting in long hours studying.

Today, we're going to look at ways you can manage these problems. We'll draw from productivity best practices as well as personal experience and offer nuggets of advice for learning to code part-time. If that sounds relevant to you, check out the five tips we're offering to see if they might help:

5 tips for successful learning

1. Set clear goals

Before embarking on a learn-to-code journey while juggling other responsibilities, you should think carefully about your motivations. Why do you want to learn to code? What kind of technologies do you want to learn? Answering these questions will help you clarify your priorities and set effective goals.

If you haven't heard of SMART goals, now is the time to learn. The acronym stands for Specific, Measurable, Achievable, Relevant, and Time-Bound, and each of these attributes contributes to successful goal-setting.

Specific

Don't be vague about what you want to achieve. Instead, answer these questions to make sure your goals are specific enough:

  • What do you need to accomplish?
  • What steps do you need to take to achieve it?

Some examples of specific learn-to-code goals might include:

Don't feel limited by these examples. But do get specific about what you're trying to learn.

Measurable

It's also important to quantify your goals. After all, if you can't measure or track your results, how will you know if you're making progress or when you've achieved your goal? Whatever metric you choose should be meaningful to your plan.

Some examples of measurable goals might be:

  • Learn the MEAN or LAMP stack sufficiently well to create and maintain two web apps
  • Learn to automate software testing with Java and Selenium, and code a full suite of unit tests
  • Learn vanilla JavaScript to establish enough of a base to learn React and complete three JavaScript coding challenges

Obviously, these goals might seem ambitious if you're just starting out. But they share the important trait of being measurable.

Achievable

To make sure you're setting achievable goals, be realistic. Ask yourself: is your objective something you can reasonably accomplish? If the answer is "no," you'll save yourself headaches by rethinking your goal.

What's achievable for a new learner depends on how much time you have, your background, and other factors. For instance, if you've already learned some HTML and CSS, the goal of learning vanilla JavaScript might be achievable. But if you've never written markup, you might want to start there before moving on to a programming language like JavaScript.

Relevant

Ask yourself: why are you setting this goal? Be clear about your motivation to ensure the goal you're setting is relevant.

If you want to be a UX designer, a full-stack web development course likely won't feel relevant because of its focus on front-end and back-end programming. But becoming an expert in HTML, CSS, and tools like Figma, Sketch, and the Adobe Suite would certainly make sense.

Time-Bound

It's important to be clear about your time horizon. Consider when you will start and finish working on the objective you've identified.

Without a clear sense of time, you run the risk of running out of motivation and quitting. Alternatively, you might grind along indefinitely without achieving your goal.

Ensuring goals are time-bound can also help you if you don't meet your deadline. For example, you might give yourself six months to learn a new tech stack for web development. If six months pass without success, you might consider whether your goal wasn't sufficiently specific, measurable, achievable, or relevant. Refine, and try again.

2. Practice time management

Without managing your schedule, learning to code part-time is going to be tough. Once you have a SMART goal or two, you can focus on time management to help you juggle all your obligations:

  • Define and prioritize your tasks: What do you need to do to support your goals (and the rest of your life)? Of these tasks, which are the most important? Break up your goals and obligations into manageable tasks and put them in a clear order of priority. Work on the most important tasks first.

  • Set a schedule and stick to it: Get a calendar app or buy the paper equivalent. Block off time to work on coding and chip away at the tasks you've defined.

  • Create incentives for completing tasks: Grinding through a long course? Buy yourself a coffee after each module or two. Making excellent progress on your SMART goal? Treat yourself to something nicer. Achieve your goal outright? Reward yourself with a dinner out, a concert, or another special indulgence.

  • Experiment with different systems and frameworks: The internet has no shortage of information about time management techniques. Look up systems like the 1-3-5 rule, autofocus, bullet journaling, don't break the chain, the Eisenhower matrix, the Pomodoro method, and others. Then put them to the test.

3. Choose courses methodically

You've decided to take a course to help you achieve your SMART goal, and you've blocked out time on your calendar for studying. Great! Unfortunately, the number of course options might be overwhelming. There are YouTube videos, online learning platforms, live classes, etc. Before picking a course randomly, *do some research.

Consider the following as you refine your options:

  • How much will it cost?
  • Does the delivery method work with your preferred learning style? (E.g., visual, auditory, physical, etc.)
  • Is the learning self-paced or scheduled, and will it work with your schedule?
  • Is the level of difficulty a good match for your experience?

If you're going to invest time and money in a course, it pays to select one methodically. If you need a place to start, check out Educative's "From Scratch" courses.

4. Code with others to maintain social connections

After committing to a goal and starting a course, you might find you have less time to socialize. But there's no reason your coding can't have a social element. You could:

  • Search Meetup.com or Facebook for study groups or other gatherings in your city and area of focus
  • Look for beginner-friendly challenges, like 100 Days of Code
  • Use Stack Overflow, Reddit, or a forum focused on your programming language to post and answer questions
  • Join a Slack workspace like the one hosted by the Python enthusiasts at PySlackers

5. Don't neglect other interests

We've shared a lot of productivity tips, so this may sound counterintuitive: sometimes you might need to spend less time coding. Whether you get together with friends or family, practice a hobby, read, or exercise, sustaining your coding efforts will be easier if you maintain some balance. Building your life around work, coding, and the basics of survival seems like a recipe for burning out. And burnout[1] seriously raises the risk of giving up on your goals.

Wrapping up and next steps

With any luck, some of the suggestions here have resonated and given you more confidence for your learn-to-code journey. Perhaps you feel ready to start working on the technical skills you'll inevitably need. If so, we’ve got resources to help you master the foundations:

  • "From Scratch" Courses
  • Introduction to Computers & Programming

You don’t need any prior knowledge, or anything installed on your desktop to get started. We’ll just start from the ground up, and you can start writing code as soon as you’re ready.

Happy learning!

Continue learning about coding on Educative

Start a discussion

What other tips do you think are helpful for part-time learners? Was this article helpful? Let us know in the comments below!
item.author: huntereducative

item.title: 3 JS 交互 | 3 JS Interactions
item.description:
亚什库马特

发表于显示一条消息。

alert("Hello");

显示一条消息，要求用户输入文本或 null。

var name = prompt("What is your name?");

注意：您可以添加一个可选的第二个参数，即输入字段的初始值。

var name = prompt("What is your name?", "John");

显示一条消息并等待用户按“确定”或“取消”。 它为 OK 返回“true”，为 Cancel 返回“false”。

var confirmation = confirm("Are you sure?");

一旦暂停，yashkumat 将无法评论或发布帖子，直到他们的暂停被解除。

一旦解除暂停，yashkumat 将能够再次发表评论和发布帖子。

一旦未发表，yashkumat 的所有帖子都将被隐藏，只有他们自己可以访问。

如果 yashkumat 没有被暂停，他们仍然可以从他们的仪表板重新发布他们的帖子。



原文:

yash kumat

Posted on Jul 5

shows a message.

alert("Hello");

2. prompt

shows a message asking the user to input text or null.

var name = prompt("What is your name?");

Note: You can add an optional second parameter, the initial value for the input field.

var name = prompt("What is your name?", "John");

3. confirm

shows a message and waits for the user to press "OK" or "Cancel". It returns "true" for OK and "false" for Cancel.

var confirmation = confirm("Are you sure?");

Mehedi Hasan Sagor - Jun 26

Once suspended, yashkumat will not be able to comment or publish posts until their suspension is removed.

Once unsuspended, yashkumat will be able to comment and publish posts again.

Once unpublished, all posts by yashkumat will become hidden and only accessible to themselves.

If yashkumat is not suspended, they can still re-publish their posts from their dashboard.
item.author: yashkumat
